funding.note: Full tuition waiver, health insurance subsidy, and stipend

faculty.name: Philip Bedient
faculty.title: Herman Brown Professor of Engineering; Director, SSPEED Center
faculty.sub_field: Surface water hydrology and flood modeling
faculty.summary: Teaches and performs research in surface water hydrology, disaster management, and flood modeling and prediction systems, including radar-based flood alert systems and regional storm-surge mitigation strategies through the SSPEED Center.

faculty.name: James Doss-Gollin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Hydroclimatic risk and water systems design
faculty.summary: Research focuses on how assumptions and uncertainties about the future shape design and investment in water systems and climate adaptation, using space-time statistical modeling, operations research, and physics-based understanding of the climate system.

faculty.name: Leonardo Duenas-Osorio
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Develops analytical and computational methods, including quantum-classical optimization algorithms, for performance assessment of structural and infrastructure systems, network reliability and resilience quantification, and seismic and hurricane risk assessment of interdependent networks.

faculty.name: Kai Gong
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research lies at the intersection of civil engineering, materials science, environmental sustainability, and energy, aiming to decarbonize cities and infrastructure through durable sustainable materials, waste encapsulation and conversion, and carbon mineralization using advanced characterization and computation.

faculty.name: Avantika Gori
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on understanding and quantifying coastal flood risk under evolving climate and landscape conditions, combining physics-based models and multivariate statistics to study tropical cyclone hazards, compound flood delineation, and interactions of coastal hazards with the built and natural environment.

faculty.name: Satish Nagarajaiah
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Professor of Mechanical Engineering
faculty.summary: Research resides at the intersection of computational structural dynamics, seismic isolation, adaptive structures and sensing, and sparse structural system identification with machine learning, developing physics-informed methods and digital twins for urban infrastructure.

faculty.name: Larissa Novelino
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Situates her research at the intersection of art and engineering, using origami to create compact deployable structures that change shape and to tailor mechanical metamaterial properties such as switchable Poisson's ratio, anisotropy, and tunable stiffness.

faculty.name: Jamie Padgett
faculty.title: Stanley C. Moore Professor in Engineering; Chair, Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research applies probabilistic methods to risk assessment of infrastructure and the quantification of resilience and sustainability, emphasizing regional structural portfolios such as bridges or oil storage tanks exposed to earthquakes, hurricanes, and deterioration.

faculty.name: Xinwu Qian
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in mathematical modeling and data-driven methods for transportation problems, focusing on transportation electrification, public transportation, and shared mobility applications, along with human mobility and urban computing.

faculty.name: Pol D. Spanos
faculty.title: Lewis B. Ryon Professor in Mechanical & Civil Engineering; Professor of Materials Science and NanoEngineering
faculty.summary: Research focuses on dynamics and vibrations of structural and mechanical systems under varied loads, particularly nonlinear and hazard/risk-exposed systems, developing analytic and numerical methods involving stochastic differential equations, Monte Carlo simulation, and signal processing.

faculty.name: Sang-ri Yi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops new computational methods for uncertainty quantification tailored for complex large-scale multi-hazard simulation models, integrating algorithmic innovations with high-performance computing to support natural-hazard risk and resilience assessment of urban infrastructure.

requirements.admission_model: Program-level admissions committee; rolling review after deadline
Program-level faculty committee; first-year fellowship then RA

funding.note: Full tuition and stipend for all full-time PhD students
Monthly stipend and full tuition waiver (fellowship)

programs.program_name: Computational Applied Mathematics and Operations Research (PhD)

requirements.admission_model: Program-level faculty committee; late apps to Jan 15

funding.note: Full tuition waiver and competitive stipend

requirements.admission_model: Program-level review; reviewed after 3 letters received

funding.note: Funding and full tuition waiver per offer letter

requirements.admission_model: Program-level faculty committee; late apps to Apr 15

funding.note: Financial assistance available (see dept financial aid page)
Fellowship award noted with admission offer

faculty.name: Perumalsamy N. Balaguru
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Balaguru specializes in reinforced and prestressed concrete structures, studying strength and serviceability behavior under static and fatigue loading, and develops innovative materials such as fiber-reinforced concrete and high-performance composites.

faculty.name: Vassiliki Demetracopoulou
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Demetracopoulou works on improving infrastructure quality in urban environments and optimizing the delivery of infrastructure systems under the risks and complexities posed by the current climate.

faculty.name: Nicole Fahrenfeld
faculty.title: Professor and Environmental Engineering Undergraduate Director
faculty.summary: Fahrenfeld studies contaminant fate and transport, environmental microbiology and organic chemistry, and water quality, with emphasis on antibiotic resistance genes, wastewater treatment effects, and microbial communities in contaminated environments.

faculty.name: Jie Gong
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Gong investigates community resilience and sustainability through geospatial big data analytics for disaster response and critical infrastructure protection, and through sensing and building data modeling for high-performance building systems.

faculty.name: Nenad Gucunski
faculty.title: Professor and Chair
faculty.summary: Gucunski specializes in seismic methods and nondestructive evaluation techniques, dynamic soil-structure interaction, and numerical modeling, applying advanced inspection methods to assess transportation infrastructure.

faculty.name: Qizhong (George) Guo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Guo specializes in hydraulics and hydrology, urban stormwater and flood management, inland and coastal water environment restoration, and green and sustainable water infrastructure.

faculty.name: Jing (Peter) Jin
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Jin researches mobile sensor data modeling and applications and vehicle characteristics and control in connected vehicle environments, with emphasis on transportation analytics and intelligent traffic management systems.

faculty.name: Yalin Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Li advances the sustainability of water and energy infrastructure through thermochemical and catalytic technologies for bioprocess engineering and resource recovery, using sustainable design approaches that weigh economic, environmental, and social tradeoffs.

faculty.name: Meiyin Liu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Liu focuses on leveraging automation and robotics to improve construction jobsite performance, especially safety, integrating expertise from surveying, electrical engineering, and construction management.

faculty.name: Xiang Liu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Liu applies artificial intelligence and big data technologies to enhance rail transportation safety and efficiency, and explores emerging technologies such as IoT and automation for connected multimodal transportation and infrastructure asset management.

faculty.name: Ali Maher
faculty.title: Professor and Director, Center for Advanced Infrastructure and Transportation
faculty.summary: Maher specializes in ground improvement, soil dynamics, infrastructure asset management, nondestructive testing, and environmental geotechnology, with emphasis on advancing transportation infrastructure and bridge asset management.

faculty.name: Monica Mazurek
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Mazurek focuses on air quality engineering and the chemical analysis of aerosol particles in the atmosphere.

faculty.name: Robert Miskewitz
faculty.title: Associate Research Professor
faculty.summary: Miskewitz works on contaminated sediment management and surface water quality in aquatic systems.

faculty.name: Husam Najm
faculty.title: Professor and Undergraduate Program Director
faculty.summary: Najm specializes in concrete materials, infrastructure repair, prestressed concrete, and earthquake engineering, including design and construction of pre-tensioned and post-tensioned concrete bridges and high-performance fiber-reinforced concrete.

faculty.name: Hani Nassif
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Nassif specializes in structural health monitoring and weigh-in-motion technologies along with reliability-based analysis of bridges, developing advanced concrete materials and monitoring systems to evaluate structural safety and performance.

faculty.name: Efthymios Nikolopoulos
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Environmental Engineering Graduate Program Director
faculty.summary: Nikolopoulos investigates hydrometeorological and hydrological extremes such as floods and droughts, aiming to improve prediction and develop mitigation strategies for these hazards and their environmental impacts.

faculty.name: Tyler Oathes
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Oathes investigates the interplay between fundamental soil behavior and the dynamic and static response of geotechnical infrastructure systems.

faculty.name: Hao Wang
faculty.title: Professor and Graduate Program Director
faculty.summary: Wang develops sustainable, resilient, and intelligent pavement and transportation infrastructure through advanced materials, multi-scale computational modeling, non-destructive testing, AI-enabled management systems, and life-cycle assessment.

faculty.name: Ruo-Qian (Roger) Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Wang develops numerical models to connect big data and decision-making in coastal engineering, natural hazards, water resources, and renewable energy systems.

faculty.name: Yook-Kong Yong
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Yong specializes in numerical modeling of bulk acoustic wave and surface acoustic wave piezoelectric resonators and filters, including their thermal, frequency, and noise characteristics.

faculty.name: Anthony J. Broccoli
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor, Environmental Sciences (GPAS Director)
faculty.summary: Broccoli studies climate dynamics, paleoclimate, and climate model feedbacks and sensitivity.

faculty.name: Chi Chen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Ecology, Evolution, and Natural Resources
faculty.summary: Chen studies ecosystem and vegetation dynamics, remote sensing, and the terrestrial carbon cycle.

faculty.name: Enrique Curchitser
faculty.title: Professor, Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Curchitser works on oceanic general circulation and coupled biophysical and numerical modeling of the Earth system.

faculty.name: Steven J. Decker
faculty.title: Associate Teaching Professor, Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Decker specializes in numerical weather prediction and forecasting and data assimilation.

faculty.name: Xiaomeng Jin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Jin studies atmospheric chemistry, air quality modeling, and satellite remote sensing of air pollution.

faculty.name: Robert E. Kopp
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor, Earth and Planetary Sciences
faculty.summary: Kopp works on paleoclimate data integration and sea level change modeling.

faculty.name: Benjamin R. Lintner
faculty.title: Professor, Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Lintner studies tropical climate dynamics and the interactions between large-scale dynamics and convection in the climate system.

faculty.name: Monica A. Mazurek
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil Engineering
faculty.summary: Mazurek focuses on air quality engineering and chemical analysis of aerosol particles.

faculty.name: Travis Miles
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Marine and Coastal Sciences
faculty.summary: Miles studies atmosphere-ocean-cryosphere interactions across multiple scales, including storm dynamics.

faculty.name: James R. Miller
faculty.title: Professor, Marine and Coastal Sciences
faculty.summary: Miller works on climate modeling and climate change, with emphasis on the polar-region hydrologic cycle.

faculty.name: Mark Miller
faculty.title: Professor, Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Miller studies cloud physics, atmospheric radiation transfer, remote sensing, and atmospheric observing systems.

faculty.name: Ying Fan Reinfelder
faculty.title: Professor, Earth and Planetary Sciences
faculty.summary: Fan Reinfelder studies hydrology and the impacts of climate change on freshwater resources.

faculty.name: Asa Rennermalm
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Geography
faculty.summary: Rennermalm studies Arctic hydroclimatology, high-latitude surface hydrology, and ice sheet dynamics.

faculty.name: David Robinson
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor, Geography
faculty.summary: Robinson studies climate and climate change, with emphasis on hemispheric snow cover kinematics and dynamics.

faculty.name: Alan Robock
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor, Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Robock studies climate change detection, attribution, and impacts, including effects of volcanic eruptions, geoengineering, and nuclear winter.

faculty.name: Mary Whelan
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Whelan studies biochemical cycling, biosphere-atmosphere interactions, and atmospheric chemistry, including carbon uptake.

faculty.name: Lili Xia
faculty.title: Assistant Research Professor, Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Xia works on climate modeling and climate change with a focus on aerosols, environment, and atmospheric pollution.

faculty.name: Donna Fennell
faculty.title: Professor and Chair
faculty.summary: Fennell researches bioremediation of contaminated environments, bioaerosols, and bioenergy.

faculty.name: Weilin Huang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Huang studies physicochemical processes governing contaminant behavior, water treatment, and soil remediation.

faculty.name: Elodie Passeport
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Passeport studies emerging contaminants, environmental remediation technologies, and stable isotope analysis of pollutant degradation.

faculty.name: John Reinfelder
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Reinfelder studies biogeochemistry in aquatic systems, including the cycling and bioaccumulation of trace elements and contaminants.

faculty.name: Nathan Yee
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Yee studies microbial geochemistry and the interactions between microorganisms and metals in the environment.

faculty.name: Lily Young
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Young studies anaerobic microbial metabolism and microbial ecology, including biodegradation of environmental contaminants.

faculty.name: Siddhartha Roy
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Roy studies water quality, public health, and environmental epidemiology, including contaminants in drinking water systems.

faculty.name: Lisa Rodenburg
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Rodenburg studies the sources, fate, and transport of organic pollutants such as PCBs in aquatic and atmospheric environments.

faculty.name: Gediminas Mainelis
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Mainelis studies biological and non-biological aerosols, including their measurement, behavior, and exposure implications.

faculty.name: Daniel Gimenez
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Gimenez studies soil science and soil physics, including soil structure and the movement of water through soils.

faculty.name: Uta Krogmann
faculty.title: Professor and Extension Specialist
faculty.summary: Krogmann studies solid waste management, green buildings, and life-cycle assessment of environmental systems.

faculty.name: Jeffra Schaefer
faculty.title: Associate Research Professor
faculty.summary: Schaefer studies environmental microbiology and biogeochemistry, including microbial transformation and cycling of metals such as mercury.

faculty.name: Robert Miskewitz
faculty.title: Research Professor
faculty.summary: Miskewitz researches contaminated sediment management and surface water quality in aquatic systems.

faculty.name: Taewon Han
faculty.title: Assistant Research Professor
faculty.summary: Han studies aerosol mechanics, including the behavior and measurement of airborne particles.

faculty.name: Karen Bemis
faculty.title: Assistant Research Professor
faculty.summary: Bemis studies geologic visualization, hydrothermal plumes, and volcanology of the seafloor.

faculty.name: Kay Bidle
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Bidle studies microbial oceanography, phytoplankton physiology and mortality, and host-virus interactions.

faculty.name: Benedict Borer
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Borer studies microbial biogeochemistry and the interactions between microbes and particles in the ocean.

faculty.name: David Bushek
faculty.title: Professor and Director, Haskin Shellfish Research Laboratory
faculty.summary: Bushek studies bivalve host-parasite interactions and oyster ecology.

faculty.name: Robert Chant
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Chant studies estuarine physics, turbulent mixing, and sediment transport in coastal and estuarine systems.

faculty.name: Paul Falkowski
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Falkowski studies the origins of life, electron transfer, biogeochemistry, and biological oceanography.

faculty.name: Heidi Fuchs
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Fuchs studies larval biophysics and predator-prey and plankton-fluid interactions in marine populations.

faculty.name: Scott Glenn
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor and Co-Director, Center for Ocean Observing Leadership
faculty.summary: Glenn develops autonomous ocean observing systems and studies extreme events in the coastal ocean.

faculty.name: Max Gorbunov
faculty.title: Research Professor
faculty.summary: Gorbunov studies coral reefs, photosynthesis, and benthic ecosystems using bio-optical approaches.

faculty.name: Joseph Gradone
faculty.title: Assistant Research Professor
faculty.summary: Gradone studies physical oceanography and water mass transport in the coastal and open ocean.

faculty.name: Thomas Grothues
faculty.title: Associate Research Professor and Director, Rutgers University Marine Field Station
faculty.summary: Grothues studies fish distribution and movement ecology using tracking technologies.

faculty.name: Ximing Guo
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Guo studies mollusk genetics, aquaculture, and selective breeding of shellfish.

faculty.name: Lee Kerkhof
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Kerkhof studies microbial diversity, biogeochemistry, and the activity of microbes in marine environments.

faculty.name: Josh Kohut
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Kohut studies coastal ocean variability and ocean observing systems.

faculty.name: Julia Levin
faculty.title: Associate Research Professor
faculty.summary: Levin works on ocean modeling and data assimilation for coastal ocean prediction.

faculty.name: Richard Lutz
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Lutz studies deep-sea hydrothermal vent ecosystems and larval ecology.

faculty.name: Travis Miles
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Miles studies physical oceanography, storm dynamics, and ice-ocean interactions.

faculty.name: Daphne Munroe
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Munroe studies invertebrate larval dispersal, population connectivity, and shellfisheries.

faculty.name: Luis Fernando Pareja Roman
faculty.title: Assistant Research Professor
faculty.summary: Pareja Roman studies coastal ocean dynamics, air-sea interaction, and offshore wind interactions.

faculty.name: Fiorella Prada
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Prada studies coral biomineralization and the effects of ocean acidification on corals.

faculty.name: Yair Rosenthal
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor and Graduate Program Director
faculty.summary: Rosenthal studies paleoceanography and the development of geochemical proxies for past ocean and climate conditions.

faculty.name: Grace Saba
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Saba studies organismal ecology and physiological responses of marine organisms to climate change and ocean acidification.

faculty.name: Danielle Santiago Ramos
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Santiago Ramos studies silicate weathering, ocean chemistry, and geochemical cycling.

faculty.name: Oscar Schofield
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor, Chair, and Co-Director, Center for Ocean Observing Leadership
faculty.summary: Schofield studies phytoplankton physiology and ecology and develops ocean observing technologies.

faculty.name: Corday Selden
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Selden studies marine biogeochemistry, microbial metabolism, and nutrient cycling in the ocean.

faculty.name: Myla Aronson
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Aronson studies patterns and drivers of global biodiversity, urban ecology, and plant ecology.

faculty.name: Christina Bergey
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Bergey uses population, evolutionary, and functional genomic approaches to study adaptation and evolution.

faculty.name: Debashish Bhattacharya
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Bhattacharya studies the genomics of algal origin and evolution.

faculty.name: Juan Bonachela
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Bonachela develops theoretical models spanning microbial ecology and evolution to terrestrial vegetation patterns.

faculty.name: Joanna Burger
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Burger studies avian and reptile behavior and ecology, salt marsh ecology, and the behavioral effects of heavy metals.

faculty.name: Chi Chen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Chen studies ecosystem and vegetation dynamics, remote sensing, and the terrestrial carbon cycle.

faculty.name: Siobain Duffy
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Duffy studies emerging viruses, experimental evolution, phylogenetics, and bacteriophages.

faculty.name: Dina Fonseca
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Fonseca studies the genetic epidemiology of disease vectors, especially introduced species.

faculty.name: Robert Goodman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Goodman studies the diversity and function of microorganisms in natural environments.

faculty.name: Julie Lockwood
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Lockwood studies conservation biology, wildlife management, and biological invasions.

faculty.name: Michael Manhart
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Manhart studies microbial communities and their ecological and evolutionary dynamics using systems biology approaches.

faculty.name: Brooke Maslo
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Maslo studies conservation biology, population dynamics, and ecosystem services, with work on bats and shorebirds.

faculty.name: Marci Meixler
faculty.title: Associate Teaching Professor
faculty.summary: Meixler studies aquatic ecology and ecological modeling of freshwater systems.

faculty.name: Peter J. Morin
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Morin studies biodiversity and ecosystem functioning, ecological networks, and community ecology.

faculty.name: Tetsuya Nakamura
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Nakamura studies fish diversity and the genetics of the fish-to-tetrapod evolutionary transition.

faculty.name: Kimberly Russell
faculty.title: Associate Teaching Professor
faculty.summary: Russell studies community ecology, urban ecology and novel ecosystems, and arthropod conservation.

faculty.name: Lena Struwe
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Struwe studies plant systematics and evolution, biogeography, ethnobotany, and biodiversity conservation.

faculty.name: Erin Vogel
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Vogel studies food acquisition and diet selection in nonhuman primates.

faculty.name: John Wiedenmann
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Wiedenmann studies marine population and ecosystem dynamics and fisheries management.

faculty.name: Rachael Winfree
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Winfree studies biodiversity, pollination ecology, and ecosystem services.

faculty.name: Dina M. Fonseca
faculty.title: Professor, Chair of the Department of Entomology, Director of the Center for Vector Biology
faculty.summary: Fonseca examines how invasive mosquito populations differ in disease-transmission capacity across space and time, affecting epidemiological risk. She develops mosquito control strategies through extension partnerships and citizen-science initiatives that use environmental DNA to detect and manage invasive species.

faculty.name: Alvaro Toledo
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Toledo investigates ticks and tick-borne pathogens, particularly Borrelia burgdorferi (the Lyme disease agent). His work examines how cholesterol and lipid rafts function in bacterial sensing and adaptation, along with the role of tick microbiomes in pathogen transmission.

faculty.name: Dana C. Price
faculty.title: Associate Research Professor
faculty.summary: Price's lab conducts functional genomic analyses of vector arthropods and their associated microorganisms to identify and surveil emerging zoonotic infectious diseases. Current work includes non-destructive pathogen sampling, reverse-vaccine approaches for invasive ticks, and spatiotemporal studies of tick-borne and arbovirus disease dynamics.

faculty.name: Chloe Hawkings
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Undergraduate Program Director
faculty.summary: Hawkings investigates the physiological mechanisms that underlie social insect behavior. Her work uses gene expression analysis to examine how insects adapt ecologically and make behavioral decisions across different life stages and castes.

faculty.name: Lena B. Brattsten
faculty.title: Research Professor
faculty.summary: Brattsten investigates biochemical resistance mechanisms in insects exposed to toxic chemicals, focusing on detoxification enzymes such as cytochrome P450s and general esterases. Her work examines physiological, behavioral, and phenological resistance mechanisms alongside the molecular genetics of insect defense systems.

faculty.name: Anne L. Nielsen
faculty.title: Associate Extension Specialist in Entomology, Graduate Program Director
faculty.summary: Nielsen develops phenological models for tree fruit and small fruit pests, integrating insect physiology and behavior into sustainable management programs. Her work emphasizes invasive species, particularly the brown marmorated stink bug (Halyomorpha halys), along with monitoring, biological control, and IPM.

faculty.name: Cesar Rodriguez-Saona
faculty.title: Extension Specialist in Entomology
faculty.summary: Rodriguez-Saona develops and implements cost-effective, reduced-risk integrated pest management practices for blueberries and cranberries. His work integrates chemical, behavioral, and biological control methods with an understanding of pest ecology and natural enemies.

faculty.name: Albrecht Koppenhofer
faculty.title: Extension Specialist in Entomology
faculty.summary: Koppenhofer develops ecologically based integrated pest management for turfgrass systems with an emphasis on sustainability and biological control. His research focuses on conservation of natural enemies and interactions among chemical, biological, and cultural control components.

faculty.name: George C. Hamilton
faculty.title: Extension Specialist in Pest Management
faculty.summary: Hamilton focuses on implementing integrated pest management programs and insect biocontrol, with current emphasis on conservation biological control for ornamental and vegetable pests. He also works on managing invasive insects including brown marmorated stink bug, spotted lanternfly, and emerald ash borer.

faculty.name: Changlu Wang
faculty.title: Extension Specialist in Entomology
faculty.summary: Wang develops urban pest management technologies by studying the biology, behavior, and ecology of pests such as cockroaches, bed bugs, ants, and termites. His extension program delivers research-based urban pest management technologies to pest management professionals and the public.

faculty.name: Andrea Gallavotti
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: His lab investigates the molecular mechanisms regulating plant architecture using maize as a model system, including how the hormone auxin regulates meristem formation and transcriptional repression in development.

faculty.name: Mark Zander
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: His work spans plant (epi)genomics, plant immunity, hormone signaling, and transcriptional regulation, focused on understanding the molecular mechanisms governing plant defense and developmental regulation.

faculty.name: Eric Lam
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: He studies mechanisms controlling programmed cell death and stress tolerance in plants and the regulation of gene expression via chromatin organization, with current work developing duckweed-based aquatic agriculture including a reference genome for Spirodela polyrhiza.

faculty.name: Pal Maliga
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: He specializes in plastid molecular biology within the broader areas of molecular biology, biotechnology, and plant genetic improvement.

faculty.name: Nilgun Tumer
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor, Director of SEBS Core Facility
faculty.summary: Her laboratory investigates the basic mechanism of toxicity of ribosome-inactivating proteins such as ricin, Shiga toxins, and trichothecene mycotoxins, using yeast models and fragment-based inhibitor discovery.

faculty.name: Ning Zhang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: She investigates the taxonomy, biodiversity, and genomics of Magnaporthales, an ascomycete fungal order containing several important plant pathogens, and develops molecular methods for rapid diagnosis of fungal pathogens.

faculty.name: James F. White, Jr.
faculty.title: Professor of Plant Pathology
faculty.summary: He specializes in symbiosis research, particularly endophytic microbes, investigating the diversity of endophytic microorganisms and their various effects on host plants.

faculty.name: Donald Y. Kobayashi
faculty.title: Professor, Department Chair
faculty.summary: His laboratory focuses on characterizing bacterial plant pathogens and the diseases they cause, especially in the greater northeast region of the US.

faculty.name: Peter Oudemans
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: He specializes in plant pathology of blueberry and cranberry, studying pathogenic fungi affecting these berry crops and using geospatial (GIS) technologies for disease management.

faculty.name: Michael Lawton
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: He studies disease resistance mechanisms and pathogen interactions, with a focus on fungal and bacterial pathogens, Fusarium head blight, toxins, and programmed cell death using functional genomics approaches.

faculty.name: C. Andrew Wyenandt
faculty.title: Extension Specialist in Vegetable Pathology
faculty.summary: His lab investigates fungicide programs for managing diseases such as phytophthora blight and anthracnose in peppers and cucurbits, evaluates control of basil downy mildew, and develops fungicide resistance management strategies.

faculty.name: Stacy Bonos
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: She develops improved turfgrass species with enhanced disease resistance and stress tolerance, and breeds switchgrass for biomass yield and bioenergy applications.

faculty.name: Gina Sideli
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Her lab focuses on classical and molecular breeding of cranberry and blueberry, integrating genomics and phenomics to understand trait genetics and implement marker-assisted or genomic selection for crop improvement.

faculty.name: Thomas J. Molnar
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: His work concentrates on the genetic improvement of hazelnuts (Corylus) and large-bracted dogwoods, including developing and characterizing genetic resistance to eastern filbert blight, the primary limiting factor of hazelnut production in eastern North America.

faculty.name: Joseph Goffreda
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: His research area, per the department directory, is the breeding of tree fruit crops including peach, nectarine, apple, and apricot.

faculty.name: Rong Di
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Her lab uses the C. elegans model to study anti-diabetic, anti-obesity, anti-Alzheimer's, anti-inflammatory, and anti-carcinogenic effects of plant extracts, and employs genetic engineering and CRISPR to develop disease-resistant and nutrition-enhanced plants.

faculty.name: Ilya Raskin
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: His research has spanned plant growth regulation, plant immunity to disease, and phytoremediation, and in the last decade has focused on plant-derived functional foods and medicines alongside international bioexploration and conservation.

faculty.name: James E. Simon
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.sub_field: New crop development and natural products
faculty.summary: He specializes in new crop development, domestication of high-value crops, non-timber forest species, and sustainable development of indigenous plant resources.

faculty.name: Lena Struwe
faculty.title: Professor and Director of Chrysler Herbarium
faculty.summary: She studies plant evolutionary biology and systematics (particularly gentians and urban weeds) alongside modern ethnobotany, with over 25 years of experience in plant diversity and botanical nomenclature across multiple continents.

faculty.name: Bingru Huang
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Her expertise is in turfgrass stress physiology, biochemistry, and molecular biology.

faculty.name: Chi-Tang Ho
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Professor Ho's research concentrates on flavor chemistry and the bioactivities of natural products, and he has authored more than 700 scholarly papers during his tenure at Rutgers.

faculty.name: Karen M. Schaich
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Dr. Schaich uses EPR spectroscopy to study free radical intermediates and their reactions, focusing on the mechanisms of lipid oxidation across contexts ranging from food preservation to cellular pathology.

faculty.name: Qingrong Huang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Food Chemistry / Food Colloids and Nanotechnology
faculty.summary: Dr. Huang's work focuses on the assembly and applications of food colloids and biopolymers, including nano- and micro-encapsulation technologies for delivering active food ingredients.

faculty.name: Michael Chikindas
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Dr. Chikindas investigates natural-derived antimicrobials and probiotics for use in human health, personal care, and agricultural applications, and has authored over 100 papers and multiple book chapters on these topics.

faculty.name: Karl R. Matthews
faculty.title: Professor, Graduate Program Director
faculty.summary: Dr. Matthews investigates microbial safety in food production and processing, focusing on antibiotic-resistant bacteria in vegetable crops and antimicrobial treatments using light and plant-derived compounds to control foodborne pathogens.

faculty.name: Donald W. Schaffner
faculty.title: Department Chair, Distinguished Professor, Extension Specialist
faculty.sub_field: Food Microbiology / Quantitative Microbial Risk Assessment
faculty.summary: Dr. Schaffner's work focuses on quantitative microbial risk assessment, predictive food microbiology, and handwashing and cross-contamination, with expertise extending to pandemic-related microbial transmission risks.

faculty.name: George M. Carman
faculty.title: Board of Governors Professor, Distinguished Professor; Founding Director, Rutgers Center for Lipid Research
faculty.summary: Dr. Carman's laboratory has contributed to understanding lipid synthesis and regulation through the isolation and characterization of key genes and their encoded enzymes, with research continuously supported by NIH grants since 1980.

faculty.name: Loredana Quadro
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Dr. Quadro is a Professor of Food Science and member of the Rutgers Center for Lipid Research whose research addresses the genetics and metabolism of fat-soluble vitamins and nutrient metabolism.

faculty.name: Diana E. Roopchand
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Nutritional Sciences / Gut Microbiota and Dietary Phytochemicals
faculty.summary: Dr. Roopchand investigates how plant phytochemicals such as dietary polyphenols interact with gut microbiota and the host to explain health benefits, and also explores cannabidiol's potential therapeutic applications for menopausal women through changes in estrogen metabolism.

faculty.name: Nuanyi Liang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Dr. Liang applies advanced analytical techniques to investigate lipid metabolites and oxylipins and their connections to chronic disease prevention, with applications in sustainable food innovation and food upcycling.

faculty.name: Tianming Yao
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Dr. Yao investigates how the structure of dietary fibers and complex polysaccharides influences gut microbiota composition and function, and how these interactions promote beneficial metabolic outcomes and microbial diversity.

faculty.name: Beverly J. Tepper
faculty.title: Professor, Graduate Program Director
faculty.summary: Dr. Tepper investigates how genetic variation in taste perception influences food preferences, dietary choices, and body weight, using genetic markers such as PROP and the oral fat sensor CD36, and studies salivary proteins in taste perception and oral health.

faculty.name: Mukund V. Karwe
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Dr. Karwe's research focuses on advanced food processing technologies, including high pressure processing, cold plasma processing, and extrusion methods for food applications.

faculty.name: Paul Takhistov
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Master of Business and Science Program Director
faculty.summary: Dr. Takhistov's group develops innovative food products using formulation engineering principles, emphasizing shelf-life extension, supply-chain stability, and contamination prevention through intelligent packaging, smart sensing, and non-thermal processing technologies.

faculty.name: Kit L. Yam
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Dr. Yam's research addresses food packaging, including controlled and modified atmosphere packaging and packaging development, as listed in the department faculty directory.

faculty.name: Carol A. Bagnell
faculty.title: Professor and Chair, Department of Animal Sciences
faculty.summary: Dr. Bagnell's lab studies how maternal factors transmitted through nursing (the 'lactocrine' mechanism) influence neonatal development, examining immediate and long-term effects of milk-borne signals on reproductive health and fertility.

faculty.name: Nicholas T. Bello
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Neuroscience of feeding behavior and obesity
faculty.summary: Dr. Bello's lab investigates mechanisms by which dietary supplements prevent metabolic consequences of obesity in rodent models, and how dysregulation of brain norepinephrine pathways contributes to aberrant eating behaviors and loss-of-control episodes in binge eating.

faculty.name: Wendie S. Cohick
faculty.title: Vice Provost for Research and Professor
faculty.sub_field: Endocrine physiology and mammary gland biology
faculty.summary: Dr. Cohick's lab examines how the insulin-like growth factor system regulates mammary gland physiology and breast cancer development, including stromal-epithelial interactions and how prenatal alcohol exposure influences lifetime breast cancer susceptibility.

faculty.name: Meli'sa S. Crawford
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Environmental physiology and toxicology of metabolic disease
faculty.summary: Dr. Crawford investigates how environmental pollutants from air and food sources contribute to metabolic diseases and gastrointestinal disorders through altered gut bacteria, intestinal inflammation, and organ crosstalk, using animal models to trace pollutant pathways.

faculty.name: Kenneth H. McKeever
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor McKeever's research focuses on equine physiology and performance, with expertise in cardiovascular responses to exercise, bridging exercise science and animal agriculture at Rutgers' Equine Science Center.

faculty.name: Troy A. Roepke
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Dean of Faculty Development and Workplace Culture (SEBS)
faculty.summary: Dr. Roepke investigates how environmental stresses and hormones affect hypothalamic physiological functions, with focus on novel membrane-initiated estrogen signaling pathways and the long-term effects of low-dose endocrine disruptor exposure on offspring.

faculty.name: Dipak K. Sarkar
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor and Director, Endocrine Program
faculty.summary: Dr. Sarkar investigates how prenatal alcohol exposure disrupts neuroimmune signaling in the hypothalamus through epigenetic pathways, causing stress dysregulation, metabolic dysfunction, immune deficiency, and increased cancer susceptibility.

faculty.name: Igor Shmarakov
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Dr. Shmarakov studies how retinoids and retinoid-interacting proteins regulate physiological functions across hepatic, adipose, pulmonary, and intestinal cells, examining disrupted retinoid signaling in lung injury and metabolic diseases.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Snyder
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Male reproductive biology and germ cell RNA biology
faculty.summary: Dr. Snyder's lab studies male germ cell RNA biology to improve reproductive health in animals and humans, focusing on novel RNA expression and nucleotide-level RNA modifications using computational, molecular, and genetic approaches.

faculty.name: Mehmet Uzumcu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Dr. Uzumcu investigates how environmental factors and endocrine-disrupting chemicals affect the female reproductive system's long-term health, examining developmental effects of pesticides and plasticizers on ovarian follicular composition and gene expression following fetal and neonatal exposures.

faculty.name: Tess Stahl
faculty.title: Assistant Extension Specialist in Animal Sciences
faculty.summary: Dr. Stahl studies prepartum nutrition changes in beef cows, sheep, and goats and their effects on offspring development, colostrum quantity and quality, and the use of byproduct feeds in livestock diets.

faculty.name: Carey A. Williams
faculty.title: Extension Specialist in Equine Management
faculty.summary: Dr. Williams investigates how nutrition impacts equine athletic performance, focusing on reducing oxidative stress and inflammation with antioxidant supplements, and examining grazing system effects on equine health and gut microbial communities.

faculty.name: Tracy G. Anthony
faculty.title: Professor of Nutritional Sciences (Graduate Program Co-Director)
faculty.sub_field: Amino acid sensing and metabolic signaling
faculty.summary: Anthony's lab studies how the body senses nutrients and converts that information into decisions about growth, stress resistance, and survival, using mouse models, cell culture, and omics approaches to examine nutrient-sensing pathways during stress and aging.

faculty.name: Paul Breslin
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor of Nutritional Sciences
faculty.sub_field: Genetics of human oral perception and nutrient intake
faculty.summary: Breslin investigates the genetic basis of human oral perception and its impact on nutrient intake, studying taste discrimination, chemesthesis, and flavor using both human research and parallel genetic studies in Drosophila.

faculty.name: Carol Byrd-Bredbenner
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor of Nutritional Sciences (Director, Graduate Program in Nutritional Sciences)
faculty.summary: Her work uses a socioecological approach to examine intrapersonal, interpersonal, and environmental factors affecting dietary choices, developing and validating theory-driven nutrition education interventions that promote healthier lifestyles.

faculty.name: Joseph L. Dixon
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Nutritional Sciences
faculty.summary: Dixon's laboratory investigates how abnormal blood lipids contribute to coronary artery disease, focusing on apoB-lipoprotein secretion from liver cells and altered lipoprotein composition in diabetes that may accelerate atherosclerosis.

faculty.name: Mackenzie Ferrante
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Nutritional Sciences
faculty.sub_field: Behavioral nutrition and children's eating behaviors
faculty.summary: Ferrante's research investigates the predictors of young children's eating behaviors early in life and develops environmental and behavioral interventions across restaurants, grocery stores, and homes to foster healthy dietary habits and prevent obesity.

faculty.name: Nurgül Fitzgerald
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Nutritional Sciences / Extension Specialist
faculty.sub_field: Type 2 diabetes prevention and health disparities
faculty.summary: Fitzgerald studies prevention of type 2 diabetes risk factors among underserved populations, examining individual, family, organizational, community, and policy-level determinants and integrating food systems analysis with lifestyle intervention research.

faculty.name: Christy Gliniak
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Nutritional Sciences
faculty.summary: Gliniak's lab studies adipose tissue biology and interorgan crosstalk during obesity and inflammatory bowel disease, with a focus on lipid regulation of cellular stress and the role of mesenteric visceral fat in metabolic disease.

faculty.name: Daniel J. Hoffman
faculty.title: Professor of Nutritional Sciences
faculty.summary: Hoffman studies how poor early-life nutrition affects long-term health, investigating metabolic adaptations in growth-stunted children and how households in transitional countries respond to economic crises through changes in food purchasing and diet.

faculty.name: Joshua W. Miller
faculty.title: Professor and Chair of Nutritional Sciences
faculty.summary: Miller investigates how B vitamins and vitamin D influence cognitive decline and neurodegenerative disease in aging populations, examining relationships among homocysteine, vitamin deficiencies, Alzheimer's disease, and hypertension.

faculty.name: Virginia Quick
faculty.title: Associate Teaching Professor of Nutritional Sciences (Director, Didactic Program in Dietetics)
faculty.summary: Quick's research examines the interplay of intrapersonal, interpersonal, and socio-environmental factors affecting health behaviors using a socioecological model, developing evidence-based interventions for obesity and disordered eating across the lifespan.

faculty.name: Harini Sampath
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Nutritional Sciences
faculty.summary: Sampath's lab investigates cellular alterations during the development of metabolic diseases such as fatty liver, cardiovascular disease, and diabetes, using transgenic mouse models and cell culture to examine lipid metabolism regulation and DNA repair pathways.

faculty.name: Sue A. Shapses
faculty.title: Professor of Nutritional Sciences (Director, Human Nutrition, Exercise and Metabolism Center)
faculty.sub_field: Endocrine regulation of obesity and bone metabolism
faculty.summary: Shapses examines mechanisms that regulate organs affected during caloric restriction and how nutrients such as protein, monounsaturated fatty acids, and vitamin D influence bone metabolism and metabolic health, particularly in aging populations.

faculty.name: Judith Storch
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor of Nutritional Sciences
faculty.summary: Storch investigates how lipids are transported and targeted within cells, examining fatty acid-binding proteins and the NPC2 cholesterol-trafficking protein using biochemical, biophysical, cellular, and whole-animal approaches relevant to obesity, cardiovascular, and lipid-storage diseases.

faculty.name: Malcolm Watford
faculty.title: Professor of Nutritional Sciences (Undergraduate Program Director)
faculty.summary: Watford's laboratory studies the role of amino acids, notably glutamine and glutamate, as substrates for gluconeogenesis and urea synthesis, examining tissue-specific glutamine metabolism and the regulation of glutaminase and glutamine synthetase across species.

requirements.admission_model: Umbrella program with tracks in Biochemistry, Cell & Developmental Biology, Pharmacology, Microbiology & Molecular Genetics, Physiology

faculty.name: Sarah Billington
faculty.title: The Gary Retelny and Family Chair of the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering, UPS Foundation Professor, Professor of Civil and Environmental Engin
faculty.summary: Billington's group studies how built environments impact human wellbeing and how human-centered engineering design can improve those impacts, developing tools and design interventions; her prior work centered on sustainable, durable construction materials including bio-based and ductile cement-based composites.

faculty.name: Alexandria Boehm
faculty.title: Richard and Rhoda Goldman Professor of Environmental Studies, Professor of Oceans and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Boehm studies pathogens in natural and engineered environments, including their sources, fate, and transport, and how they are transmitted to humans through water and contaminated surfaces, with the goal of designing interventions to reduce disease burden; she also works broadly on coastal water quality and nitrogen fate.

faculty.name: Ronaldo Borja
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Borja works in computational mechanics, geomechanics, and geosciences, developing strain localization and failure models for soils and rocks, modeling coupled solid deformation and fluid flow in porous materials, and finite element modeling of faulting and fracturing.

faculty.name: Jenna Davis
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering, of Environmental Social Sciences and Higgins-Magid Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute
faculty.summary: Davis heads the Stanford Program on Water, Health & Development, with research and teaching focused at the nexus of water, economic development, and public health, particularly in low- and middle-income countries, with field research in more than 20 countries.

faculty.name: Gregory Deierlein
faculty.title: John A. Blume Professor in the School of Engineering
faculty.summary: Deierlein's research focuses on improving limit-states design of constructed facilities through nonlinear structural analysis methods and performance-based design criteria, including seismic behavior of steel and reinforced concrete structures and fracture in welded steel connections.

faculty.name: Sarah Fletcher
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and Center Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: The Fletcher lab advances water resources management to promote resilient and equitable responses to a changing world, integrating methods from hydrology, policy analysis, and data science to inform environmental decision-making.

faculty.name: Oliver Fringer
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and of Oceans
faculty.summary: Fringer develops and applies numerical models and high-performance computational techniques to study fundamental processes influencing the dynamics of the coastal ocean, rivers, lakes, and estuaries.

faculty.name: Catherine Gorle
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and, by courtesy, of Mechanical Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Predictive flow simulation for sustainable cities
faculty.summary: Gorle's research focuses on developing predictive flow simulations to support the design of sustainable buildings and cities, including coupling large- and small-scale models and experiments to quantify uncertainties in urban wind and environmental flows.

faculty.name: Lynn Hildemann
faculty.title: Wayne Loel Professor of Sustainability and Senior Associate Dean for Education
faculty.summary: Hildemann's research addresses the sources and dispersion of airborne particulate matter indoors and the assessment of human exposure to air pollutants.

faculty.name: Mark Z. Jacobson
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment and at the Precourt Institute for Energy
faculty.summary: Jacobson develops and applies three-dimensional atmosphere-biosphere-ocean computer models to simulate air pollution, weather, climate, and renewable energy, and has developed roadmaps to transition states and countries to 100% clean, renewable energy.

faculty.name: Rishee Jain
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Jain's research develops data-driven and socio-technical solutions to sustainability problems facing the urban built environment, at the intersection of civil engineering, data analytics, and social science, including commercial building energy usage and urban sustainability planning.

faculty.name: Peter K. Kitanidis
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Kitanidis develops methods for interpolation and inverse problems using observations and mathematical models of flow and transport, studying dilution and mixing in heterogeneous geologic formations, in situ pollutant decay, and hydrologic forecasting.

faculty.name: Christian Linder
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Linder directs the Computational Mechanics of Materials Lab, developing computational methods to understand micromechanically originated multi-scale and multi-physics mechanisms in solid materials undergoing large deformations and fracture, with applications including sustainable energy storage materials.

faculty.name: Meagan Mauter
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil & Environmental Engineering, of Photon Science, Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment and at the Precourt Instit
faculty.summary: Mauter directs the Water and Energy Efficiency for the Environment Lab (WE3Lab), aiming to provide sustainable water supply in a carbon-constrained world through innovation in water treatment technology, optimization of water management, and redesign of water policy.

faculty.name: Eduardo Miranda
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Miranda specializes in structural engineering with emphasis on performance-based earthquake engineering, studying how structures respond to earthquakes and developing ways to design and build structures with improved seismic performance.

faculty.name: William Mitch
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Mitch employs a fundamental understanding of environmental chemistry to study contaminants in water, including disinfection byproduct formation and control relevant to drinking water and potable water reuse.

faculty.name: Stephen Monismith
faculty.title: Obayashi Professor in the School of Engineering and Professor of Oceans
faculty.summary: Monismith applies fluid mechanics principles to flow processes in rivers, lakes, estuaries, and oceans, with particular interest in ecological impacts, studying estuarine hydrodynamics and mixing, flows over coral reefs, and stratified turbulence.

faculty.name: Khalid Osman
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and Center Fellow, by courtesy, at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Osman uses mixed quantitative-qualitative methods to assess public perceptions of water infrastructure and conservation, focusing on operationalizing equity in water-sector infrastructure, water affordability, and stakeholder engagement for environmental justice.

faculty.name: Iro Armeni
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Machine perception for the built environment
faculty.summary: Armeni works at the intersection of architecture, civil engineering, and visual machine perception, developing data-driven methods that learn from real-world visual data to generate, predict, and simulate sustainable, inclusive, and adaptive built environments.

faculty.name: Christine M Baker
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and, by courtesy, of Oceans
faculty.summary: Baker studies processes at the land-ocean interface, integrating laboratory experimentation, numerical modeling, and remote sensing to understand coastal hydrodynamics and inform predictions of coastal water quality, shoreline evolution, and coastal resiliency.

faculty.name: Yaochun Yu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Yu's research integrates high-resolution mass spectrometry, meta-omics, molecular microbiology, and computational modeling to identify functional microbes, genes, and enzymes driving chemical biotransformation, aiming to develop biosolutions for bioremediation and resource recovery.

faculty.name: Jack Baker
faculty.title: William Alden Campbell and Martha Campbell Professor and Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Baker uses probabilistic and statistical tools to quantify and manage disaster risk and resilience, contributing to risk analysis of spatially distributed systems, characterization of earthquake ground motions, and simulation of post-disaster recovery.

funding.note: Research Assistantships fund salary, tuition allowance, health subsidy
Generally full financial support for first five years

Graduate Teaching/Research Associateships and fellowships available

faculty.name: Patrick Bassal
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Civil, Environmental and Geodetic Engineering
faculty.summary: Combines nonlinear dynamic geotechnical modeling with subsurface characterization, geostatistics, and remote sensing (InSAR, LiDAR) to understand ground failures from natural hazards. Conducted post-earthquake reconnaissance including the 2023 Turkiye-Syria earthquakes.

faculty.name: Min Liew
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering
faculty.summary: Specializes in permafrost and cold regions geotechnics, climate change-related geohazards, and climate-resilient sustainable civil infrastructure. Uses numerical modeling of coupled thermal-hydraulic-mechanical physics validated with laboratory, field, and sensing methods.

faculty.name: Halil Sezen
faculty.title: Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering
faculty.summary: Researches structural analysis, design, and performance assessment of structures under extreme loading conditions, including dynamic behavior and collapse of buildings and rehabilitation of concrete buildings and bridges. Has published over 150 peer-reviewed articles with funding from NSF, FHWA, and DOE.

faculty.name: Abdollah Shafieezadeh
faculty.title: Professor, Civil, Environmental and Geodetic Engineering; Abba G. Lichtenstein Professorship in Civil Engineering
faculty.summary: Leads the RAMSIS Lab advancing uncertainty quantification, reliability, resilience, and life-cycle performance assessment methods for structural and infrastructure systems ranging from buildings and bridges to power grids and flood defenses, addressing both acute hazards and chronic deterioration.

faculty.name: Lisa Burris
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering
faculty.summary: Focuses on increasing the sustainability of concrete construction through novel supplementary and alternative cementitious materials for traffic infrastructure, characterizing material properties and linking hydration to concrete strength and durability.

faculty.name: Ethan Kubatko
faculty.title: Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering
faculty.summary: Develops and applies computational models of fluid flow and transport processes, with applications from hurricane storm surge simulation to sea-level rise impacts on estuarine systems and tidal turbine performance. Directs the Computational Hydrodynamics and Informatics Laboratory.

faculty.name: Benjamin Coifman
faculty.title: Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering; Professor, Electrical and Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Works on intelligent transportation systems, developing new approaches to traffic surveillance that lead to better traffic control, spanning traffic flow theory and driver dynamics while bridging academic research with practical implementation.

faculty.name: Rabi Mishalani
faculty.title: Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering
faculty.summary: Applies probabilistic modeling, statistical inference, experimental design, and optimization to public transportation planning and operations, real-time network monitoring, and infrastructure resilience decision-making, using OSU's campus transit system as a living laboratory.

faculty.name: Andre Carrel
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering; Associate Professor, Knowlton School of Architecture
faculty.summary: Directs the Travel Behavior Research Group, studying the dynamics of travel behavior, travelers' adaptation to experienced service quality, and the impact of emerging mobility technologies (autonomous, shared, alternative-fuel) on travel demand to inform sustainable transportation policy.

faculty.name: Kelsea Best
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering; Assistant Professor, Knowlton School of Architecture
faculty.summary: Investigates how climate change interacts with human societies and infrastructure, and how adaptation strategies can be implemented equitably, using computational methods such as machine learning and agent-based modeling of coupled human-natural systems.

faculty.name: Jim Stagge
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering
faculty.summary: Directs the Hydrologic Extremes Research Laboratory, investigating the frequency and severity of droughts and floods, climate change impacts on water resources, and management strategies to protect society and the environment.

faculty.name: Linda Weavers
faculty.title: Professor, Civil, Environmental and Geodetic Engineering; John C. Geupel Chair, College of Engineering
faculty.summary: Develops water and hazardous waste treatment technologies, investigating ultrasound for treatment of emerging contaminants such as PFAS, harmful algal bloom control, and membrane defouling. Has secured over $35 million in sponsored research funding.

faculty.name: John Lenhart
faculty.title: Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering; Associate Chair
faculty.summary: Studies reactions and processes at important natural and engineered interfaces to understand contaminant fate, combining experiments with mathematical modeling on topics including cyanotoxins, microplastics, arsenic in Ohio groundwater, and rare earth element recovery from acid mine drainage.

faculty.name: Karen Dannemiller
faculty.title: Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering; Professor, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Directs the Indoor Environmental Quality research group, studying chemical and microbial processes in indoor environments that impact human health, with funding from NSF, NIH, HUD, and NASA.

faculty.name: Rongjun Qin
faculty.title: Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering; Professor, Electrical and Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Develops computational methods for accurately measuring static and moving objects in urban contexts using aerial/UAV imagery, LiDAR, and satellite multispectral/hyperspectral images. Author of the RSP and MSP software tools for reconstructing 3D information from 2D images.

faculty.name: Alper Yilmaz
faculty.title: Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering; Professor, Computer Science and Engineering (courtesy)
faculty.summary: Leads the Photogrammetric Computer Vision Lab, focusing on biomimetic navigation systems for unmanned systems, anomaly mining in multi-physics data, and learning geospatial information for scene understanding, with over $13M in extramural funding and 190+ publications.

faculty.name: Lei Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil, Environmental and Geodetic Engineering
faculty.summary: Specializes in acquiring, analyzing, and interpreting geodetic data, developing high-precision GNSS positioning algorithms with applications to tectonic and geodynamic deformations and interactions between environmental changes and the solid Earth.
