faculty.name: Douglas Alsdorf
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates tropical wetland systems in the Congo and Amazon Basins, focusing on water flux dynamics and their connections to flooding, ecology, and climate change using satellite-based measurements of water surface elevations.

faculty.name: Loren Babcock
faculty.title: Professor and Director of the Orton Geological Museum
faculty.summary: Studies the early history of multicellular animals (notably trilobites), biotic changes across the Ediacaran-Cambrian transition, Cambrian global chronostratigraphy, and processes of fossilization.

faculty.name: Michael Barton
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates magma generation, movement, and evolution in the Earth's interior using petrology, thermodynamics, geochemistry, and geophysics, with projects on Iceland volcanism, crustal formation, magma degassing, and mantle source characteristics.

faculty.name: Emilie Beaudon
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates paleoclimate signals preserved in ice cores from polar and alpine regions, using geochemical analysis of mineral dust to examine changes in pollution transport pathways, atmospheric contamination, paleofire histories, and climate variability.

faculty.name: David Cole
faculty.title: Professor, Ohio Research Scholar
faculty.summary: Investigates geochemical processes including reaction mechanisms in minerals and fluids, isotopic climate proxies, CO2 sequestration in geological formations, and molecular-level behavior of carbon-bearing fluids in nanopores; directs the SEMCAL subsurface characterization laboratory.

faculty.name: Ann Cook
faculty.title: Professor, Director of Undergraduate Studies
faculty.summary: Investigates subseafloor natural gas hydrate systems using petrophysics, geophysics, and mathematical modeling, focusing on identifying and quantifying gas hydrate deposits, with additional work on marine geology and carbon sequestration.

faculty.name: Brendan Crowell
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of seismology and geodesy to advance earthquake and tsunami early warning systems and hazard mitigation, with expertise in seismogeodesy and natural hazards assessment.

faculty.name: Thomas Darrah
faculty.title: Professor, Director of the Office of Industry Partnerships and Commercialization
faculty.summary: Investigates geological processes controlling fluid migration in Earth's crust and mantle, develops geochemical techniques for energy exploration, and applies isotope geochemistry to assess environmental and health impacts of energy activities; leads the Water, Energy, and Life Laboratory (WELL).

faculty.name: Melisa Diaz
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Applies geochemical methods to understand physical systems and their interactions with biology and society, focusing on salt and nutrient transport in Antarctic and Arctic environments as well as urban geochemistry and environmental justice.

faculty.name: Michael Durand
faculty.title: Professor, Associate Director for Graduate Students
faculty.summary: Investigates land surface hydrology using spaceborne remote sensing to address questions in snow hydrology and fluvial hydrology, including changes in snowmelt runoff patterns and remote estimation of river discharge.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Griffith
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses non-traditional stable isotopes (Ca, Sr) in combination with traditional paleoclimate proxies to study biogeochemical cycling in modern and ancient oceans, reconstructing past environmental conditions.

faculty.name: W. Ashley Griffith
faculty.title: Professor, Associate Director for Administration
faculty.summary: Investigates structural geology and rock mechanics with a focus on fracture behavior in the Earth's crust, examining how fractures influence subsurface fluid movement, earthquake hazards, and engineering challenges.

faculty.name: Andrea G. Grottoli
faculty.title: College of Arts and Sciences Distinguished Professor of Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies the drivers of coral resilience under climate change, develops blue technology for coral restoration, and reconstructs past oceanographic conditions using coral skeletal records; directs the NSF-funded Coral Bleaching Research Coordination Network.

faculty.name: Ian Howat
faculty.title: School Director, ENGIE-Axium Professor, Distinguished University Scholar
faculty.summary: A glaciologist whose research centers on ice sheet and glacier dynamics, hydrology, and remote sensing of Earth's surface processes; internationally recognized for satellite-based mapping of ice flow and elevation change in polar regions.

faculty.name: Motomu Ibaraki
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates water contamination and insufficient water supplies caused by human activities and their impacts on health and ecosystems, including hydrological influences on disease transmission, wetland hydrology using radar altimetry, and water-energy sustainability.

faculty.name: Jill Leonard-Pingel
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: An invertebrate paleontologist examining how the environment shapes biological communities across geologic timescales and in modern conservation contexts, with current focus on Neogene mollusks affected by the formation of the Isthmus of Panama and changing reef communities.

faculty.name: Steven Lower
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works across geomicrobiology, mineralogy, and biophysics, with recent projects on biomineralizing magnetic particles, cancer caused by nanoscale minerals, acidophilic sulfide-oxidizing bacteria, and infectious biofilms on medical implants.

faculty.name: Alex Michaud
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates microbial biogeochemistry at the intersection of microbial ecology and glaciology, focusing on how the loss of ice impacts the role of microorganisms in globally relevant element cycles (C, Fe, S) in polar and alpine regions.

faculty.name: Joachim Moortgat
faculty.title: Professor, Director of the BuckAI Observatory
faculty.summary: Develops computational methods for multiphase flow in fractured porous media with applications to CO2 sequestration and hydrogen reservoirs, and applies deep learning to satellite imagery for river mapping, coastal bathymetry, and deforestation tracking.

faculty.name: Matthew Saltzman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: His research group investigates the role of the carbon cycle in climate change and biotic events over the past half billion years of Earth history, using stable isotope analysis of sedimentary rocks to understand organic matter burial and continental weathering rates.

faculty.name: Lonnie Thompson
faculty.title: Professor, Distinguished University Scholar
faculty.summary: Specializes in ice core paleoclimatology, extracting climate histories from tropical and subtropical ice fields in the Andes, Himalayas, and Kilimanjaro; his work has advanced understanding of El Nino and monsoon patterns and documented glacier retreat outside the range of climate variability for several millennia.

faculty.name: Zhengyu Liu
faculty.title: Max Thomas Professor of Climate Dynamics, Atmospheric Sciences Program Director
faculty.summary: Models the global climate system and ocean-atmosphere-land interaction, with application to climate change and climate variability. His research spans climate dynamics from seasonal to interdecadal timescales, past glacial-interglacial cycles, and future warming scenarios.

faculty.name: David Bromwich
faculty.title: Research Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates global climate change in polar regions using climate models and atmospheric reanalyses, emphasizing local and tropical influences. He directs development of polar versions of the WRF and MPAS regional weather prediction models and leads the Arctic System Reanalysis project on the coupled atmosphere-land-ocean-sea ice system.

faculty.name: Man-Yau ("Joseph") Chan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Data Assimilation and Earth System Prediction
faculty.summary: A statistical meteorologist focused on improving Earth system forecasts through ensemble data assimilation methods that integrate measurements with computer models. His work addresses error growth in Earth system models to enhance weather, ocean, and land forecasting systems for disaster mitigation.

faculty.name: Jana Houser
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Director of Undergraduate Studies
faculty.summary: Investigates tornado formation and evolution using mobile radar observations, with emphasis on how rotation develops within thunderstorms. Her work also examines how topography and land cover influence tornado intensity and path.

faculty.name: Bryan Mark
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Glacier Environmental Change research group at the Byrd Polar and Climate Research Center, studying climate-glacier-hydrologic dynamics over different time scales with emphasis on climate change, glaciers, and water in mountain regions. Field research spans the Peruvian Andes, Nevada's Great Basin, the Hindu Kush Himalaya, and Africa.

faculty.name: Alvaro Montenegro
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Integrates paleoclimate modeling with archaeological and biogeographic questions, using agent-based simulations to investigate historical human mobility. His research examines scenarios such as the prehistoric colonization of remote Oceania and how climate variability influenced past human dispersal.

faculty.name: Ellen Mosley-Thompson
faculty.title: Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: Analyzes chemical and physical properties preserved in ice cores from polar ice sheets and high mountain glaciers to reconstruct Earth's climate history. She has led multiple expeditions to Antarctica and Greenland and has shown that current climate conditions exceed the range of natural variability of the past 2,000 years.

faculty.name: Steven Quiring
faculty.title: Professor, Promotion and Tenure Chair
faculty.summary: A climatologist specializing in hydroclimatology, synoptic climatology, and applying climate data to societally relevant problems. His research focuses on land-atmosphere interactions to improve drought prediction and on assessing the impacts of hurricanes, thunderstorms, and winter storms on power infrastructure.

faculty.name: Aaron Wilson
faculty.title: State Climatologist of Ohio; Assistant Professor, Ag Weather and Climate Field Specialist
faculty.summary: An atmospheric scientist at the Byrd Polar and Climate Research Center who assesses hydrologic conditions throughout Ohio and coordinates the state's contributions to the U.S. Drought Monitor. His work emphasizes how changing weather extremes impact urban and rural communities.

faculty.name: Lonnie Thompson
faculty.title: Distinguished University Professor, School of Earth Sciences; Senior Research Scientist, Byrd Polar and Climate Research Center
faculty.summary: Specializes in ice core paleoclimatology, developing lightweight solar-powered drilling equipment to recover climate records from tropical and subtropical ice fields in the Andes, Himalayas, and Kilimanjaro. His decades of glacier observations document worldwide glacier retreat and show current warming exceeds natural climate variability over millennia.

faculty.name: Douglas Alsdorf
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies tropical wetlands and water systems in the Congo and Amazon Basins using satellite measurements to understand water flux, flooding, ecology, and global change impacts.

faculty.name: Michael Bevis
faculty.title: Professor, Ohio Eminent Scholar
faculty.summary: Uses geodetic techniques to investigate crustal motion, earthquake deformation, mountain building, sea-level change, and atmospheric water vapor, with GPS networks established across multiple continents.

faculty.name: Brendan Crowell
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on seismogeodesy and early warning systems, studying earthquakes and natural hazards through integrated geophysical and geodetic approaches.

faculty.name: Michael Durand
faculty.title: Professor, Associate Director for Graduate Students
faculty.sub_field: Remote sensing of snow and rivers
faculty.summary: Investigates land surface hydrology using spaceborne remote sensing, with emphasis on snow hydrology and fluvial hydrology, including snow-runoff dynamics, snow microstructure effects on satellite measurements, and river discharge estimation.

faculty.name: Demian Gomez
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: A geophysicist and geometrical geodesist researching regional and global reference frame realizations, which he uses to investigate geodynamic processes such as the earthquake cycle and periodic loading and unloading of the Earth's crust.

faculty.name: Shin-Chan Han
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Analyzes space geodetic measurements and models geophysical processes governing mass changes on Earth and terrestrial planets, with applications to the solid Earth, water systems, ice mass, oceans, and planetary bodies.

faculty.name: Ian Howat
faculty.title: School Director, ENGIE-Axium Professor, Distinguished University Scholar
faculty.summary: Studies ice sheet dynamics and glacier behavior using satellite-based Earth observation, focusing on how polar regions respond to climate change through advanced mapping technologies.

faculty.name: Joachim Moortgat
faculty.title: Professor, Director of the BuckAI Observatory
faculty.sub_field: AI-powered Earth observation and computational Earth science
faculty.summary: Works on computational Earth science, combining finite element methods for multiphase flow in porous media with AI-powered Earth observation using deep learning and physics-informed neural networks, applied to problems such as CO2 sequestration and satellite-based environmental monitoring.

faculty.name: In-Young Yeo
faculty.title: Professor (on leave)
faculty.summary: Listed under the Geodetic Science division with research interests in geodetic science and water, climate and the environment; currently on leave per the OSU directory.

faculty.name: Rongjun Qin
faculty.title: Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering; Professor, Electrical and Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Specializes in computational image analysis and 3D vision, focusing on accurate localization and detection of objects using aerial/UAV imagery, LiDAR, and satellite data for urban measurement and monitoring applications.

faculty.name: Lei Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil, Environmental and Geodetic Engineering
faculty.sub_field: GNSS positioning and geodetic data analysis
faculty.summary: Specializes in geodetic data acquisition and analysis, including high-precision static and kinematic GNSS positioning algorithms and applications, tectonic and geodynamic deformations, and the interaction between environmental changes and the solid Earth.

faculty.name: Alper Yilmaz
faculty.title: Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering; Professor, Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Photogrammetric computer vision and geospatial AI
faculty.summary: Researches biomimetic navigation systems for unmanned systems, mining anomalies in multi-physics and multi-dimensional data for surveillance, and learning geospatial information for scene understanding, using deep learning, computer vision, photogrammetry, and indoor positioning.

faculty.name: Nicholas Basta
faculty.title: Co-Director, Professor
faculty.summary: Basta specializes in risk-based soil environmental chemistry emphasizing bioavailability, exposure, and impact to human, agronomic, and ecological receptors. His work also covers soil remediation technologies and environmental risk assessment of land-applied agricultural and industrial by-products.

faculty.name: Gil Bohrer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Bohrer works on atmospheric modeling and biosphere-atmosphere interactions, focusing on eddy flux measurements, forest canopy structure and function, forest fire modeling, ecological dispersal, and land surface heterogeneity.

faculty.name: Michael Durand
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Durand studies land surface hydrology processes, using data assimilation methods and spaceborne remote sensing measurements to address open questions in snow hydrology and fluvial hydrology.

faculty.name: Rachel Gabor
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Gabor's research focuses on watershed hydrology, examining water movement and management within watershed systems.

faculty.name: Steven W. Lyon
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Lyon investigates how landscapes and water interact across scales, focusing on improving water quality management and ecosystem functionality. His work combines hydrological process analysis with community engagement to address coupled climate and land-use change impacts on water resources.

faculty.name: Jay Martin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Martin investigates ecosystems for waste utilization and treatment, sustainable agriculture, ecosystem modeling, riparian ecosystem restoration, wetlands, and aquaculture.

faculty.name: Virginia Rich
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Rich investigates microbial biogeochemistry using molecular ecology techniques to understand how microbes respond to anthropogenic environmental change. Her flagship research examines permafrost collapse in Arctic Sweden and microbially-mediated ecosystem functions at the Catalina-Jemez Critical Zone Observatory.

faculty.name: Matthew Sullivan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Sullivan studies the co-evolution of microbes and viruses in environmental systems, emphasizing how marine phages influence microbial-driven global biogeochemistry. His work uses genomics and experimental approaches to investigate phage-host interactions in cultured and natural populations.

faculty.name: Jim Ippolito
faculty.title: Dr. Rattan Lal Endowed Professor
faculty.summary: Ippolito studies environmental soil health across ecosystems, examining connections between soil nutrients, microbiology, and physical properties. His work explores how these factors influence ecosystem sustainability, food security, climate resilience, and environmental health.

faculty.name: Scott Demyan
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Demyan focuses on soil organic matter and mineral stabilization mechanisms, inorganic carbon dynamics, and ex situ mineral carbonation as a sequestration method, with emphasis on carbon permanence in soil and environmental mineralogy.

faculty.name: Richard Dick
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Dick studies soil microbial ecology, specifically microbial diversity and function that affects ecosystem services and sustainability, and biogeochemical processes related to microorganisms.

faculty.name: James Stagge
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Stagge's work focuses on understanding hydrologic extremes with the goal of decreasing drought vulnerability. His prior research analyzed drought risk patterns across Europe through an EU-funded initiative.

faculty.name: Ryan Winston
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Winston investigates water resource management with particular focus on urban stormwater and the relationships between stormwater control measures and their broader impacts on society, including water quality improvement and climate change mitigation.

faculty.name: Steven Quiring
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Quiring is a climatologist studying hydroclimatology, synoptic climatology, and applying climate data to solve societally-relevant problems. His work focuses on land-atmosphere interactions for drought prediction and modeling weather impacts on power infrastructure.

faculty.name: Yanlan Liu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Liu studies plant responses to climate variations and drought by combining physically-based models and data-driven analytics across ecosystems ranging from wetlands, forests, and deserts, at scales from a single site to continents.

faculty.name: Alexander Michaud
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Michaud is a microbial biogeochemist conducting research at the intersection of microbial ecology, biogeochemistry, and glaciology, focusing on how microorganisms contribute to biogeochemical cycles in ice-affected regions.

faculty.name: Sami Khanal
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Khanal's research interests span agroecosystems, climate change, mathematical modeling, remote sensing, and water quality.

faculty.name: Kaiguang Zhao
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Zhao employs geospatial tools and modeling approaches to map, monitor, model, and manage terrestrial environments and examine ecosystem responses to disturbances and climate change, emphasizing remote sensing of ecosystem structure and function.

faculty.name: John Lenhart
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Lenhart's work focuses on environmental chemistry, surface chemistry, colloidal processes, and contaminant fate and transport, exploring how contaminants move through and interact with environmental surfaces and systems.

faculty.name: Darren Drewry
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Drewry's research examines interactions of agricultural systems with the environment, spanning ecohydrology, terrestrial biophysics, and land-atmosphere interactions, using simulation and sensing techniques to improve vegetation resilience.

faculty.name: Kerry Ard
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Environmental and Natural Resource Sociology
faculty.summary: Ard examines connections between environmental policy, inequality, and public health outcomes. Her work investigates how social and economic systems shape environmental risks and their effects on population health.

faculty.name: Nicholas Basta
faculty.title: Professor of Soil and Environmental Chemistry
faculty.summary: Basta's research focuses on environmental soil chemistry, examining biogeochemical processes affecting heavy metal bioavailability, human health risks in soil-water systems, and remediation technologies for contaminated soils.

faculty.name: Ramiro Berardo
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental and Natural Resources Policy
faculty.summary: Berardo studies how policy actors interact when addressing environmental problems, examining how social capital forms among stakeholders and affects their support for policy responses.

faculty.name: Jeremy Brooks
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Brooks is an environmental social scientist investigating sustainability through cultural evolution theory, examining dynamics between individuals and the socio-cultural, physical, and natural environments.

faculty.name: Julia DeFeo
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Silviculture & Forest Ecology
faculty.summary: DeFeo is a silviculturist and fire ecologist studying drivers of resilience in forest ecosystems, with current focus on biodiversity in two-aged silvicultural systems and Appalachian fuel complexes.

faculty.name: M. Scott Demyan
faculty.title: Associate Professor in Soil and Environmental Mineralogy
faculty.sub_field: Soil organic matter and carbon dynamics
faculty.summary: Demyan's work focuses on soil organic matter and mineral stabilization mechanisms, inorganic carbon dynamics, and ex situ mineral carbonation as a carbon sequestration method.

faculty.name: Alia Dietsch
faculty.title: Associate Professor in Parks, Protected Areas, Natural Resources Management
faculty.summary: Dietsch applies social science theories to conservation decision-making and natural resource management, focusing on how human thought affects public support for management actions and behaviors related to wildlife and environmental issues.

faculty.name: Rachel Gabor
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Watershed Hydrology
faculty.summary: Gabor's research examines how environmental changes affect wetland ecosystems, with particular focus on microbial communities and mercury cycling in response to dissolved organic matter variations.

faculty.name: Robert Gates
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Wildlife Ecology and Management
faculty.summary: Gates investigates population-habitat relationships of migratory waterfowl, marsh birds, shorebirds, and upland game birds, along with wildlife diseases and threatened species conservation.

faculty.name: Shoshanah Inwood
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Inwood examines agriculture, environment, and society at the intersection of community development, focusing on health and well-being in the food and agriculture sector, farm growth, and food system resilience.

faculty.name: Douglas Jackson-Smith
faculty.title: Professor and Kellogg Endowed Chair of Agroecosystem Management
faculty.summary: Jackson-Smith investigates the social, economic, and environmental dynamics of agricultural systems, focusing on crop-livestock integration, farmer conservation behaviors, and participatory research methods.

faculty.name: Jeffrey Jacquet
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Rural Sociology
faculty.summary: Jacquet is a rural and natural resource sociologist studying energy development impacts, including social consequences from renewable and fossil fuel projects, plus rural community development and environmental change.

faculty.name: Jim Ippolito
faculty.title: Professor, Rattan Lal Endowed Professor of Soil Health and Soil Fertility
faculty.summary: Ippolito's research focuses on soil health and fertility, examining connections between soil nutrients, microbial activity, and contaminants to improve ecosystem sustainability and food security.

faculty.name: Stephen Matthews
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Wildlife Landscape Ecology
faculty.summary: Matthews studies landscape ecology and biodiversity responses to environmental change, focusing on climate and land-use impacts on tree and bird species across the Eastern United States and forest management effects on vertebrate habitat use.

faculty.name: Sayeed Mehmood
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Natural Resources Economics
faculty.summary: Mehmood applies economic and social science theories to understand human behavior related to natural resources and environmental management, with recent focus on forest landowner economics, ecosystem restoration, and biomass energy policy.

faculty.name: Risa Pesapane
faculty.title: Assistant Professor in Ecosystem and Wildlife Health
faculty.summary: Pesapane is a community disease ecologist with an emphasis on wildlife health and vector-borne disease, focusing on understanding how diseases spread through wildlife populations and ecosystems.

faculty.name: William Peterman
faculty.title: Associate Professor in Wildlife Ecology and Management
faculty.summary: Peterman studies landscape ecology and conservation biology, examining how climate change, habitat alteration, and urbanization affect animal populations, biodiversity, and dispersal across landscapes using field, experimental, and quantitative modeling approaches.

faculty.name: Lauren Pintor
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Aquatic Ecology and Associate Director of Undergraduate Education
faculty.summary: Pintor's work examines how species interactions structure ecological communities and ecosystem function, with particular focus on biological invasions and the mechanisms driving predator-prey dynamics.

faculty.name: Christopher Tonra
faculty.title: Associate Professor in Avian Wildlife Ecology and Chair, Schiermeier Olentangy River Wetland Research Park
faculty.summary: Tonra examines how individuals interact with their environments, focusing on avian systems, integrating behavior, physiology, and ecology to understand mechanisms shaping life history and population responses to environmental change.

faculty.name: Nicole Sintov
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Behavior, Decision Making and Sustainability
faculty.summary: Sintov is an environmental psychologist studying psychological and social factors influencing sustainable behavior, developing behavior-change interventions, and investigating factors affecting intervention success, particularly regarding household energy use and technology adoption.

programs.program_name: PhD in Civil and Environmental Engineering

funding.note: Full-time PhD students in School of Engineering often receive tuition scholarship

faculty.name: Laurie Gaskins Baise
faculty.title: Professor and Chair, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Works on natural hazards such as earthquakes and extreme wind, linking predictive models with observational data. Specializes in earthquake site response, regional seismic hazard mapping, and remotely sensed imagery for post-disaster damage assessment.

faculty.name: C. Andrew Ramsburg
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering; Dean of Undergraduate Education, School of Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies transport and reaction processes occurring in multiphase systems with applications to environmental contamination, including emulsion delivery in porous media and the fate of emerging contaminants in biological treatment systems.

faculty.name: Wayne Chudyk
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research centers on fiber optic sensors, groundwater monitoring, and water treatment for both drinking water and wastewater, with a focus on drinking water quality and monitoring of toxic materials.

faculty.name: Luis Dorfmann
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Develops mathematical models of material behavior, including nonlinear magneto- and electromechanical interactions and the biomechanics of soft materials, with applications in finite strain theory and constitutive modeling of highly deformable materials.

faculty.name: John Durant
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research focuses on air pollution monitoring, mobile monitoring platforms, air pollution modeling, and ambient air quality, together with exposure assessment for health studies.

faculty.name: David Gute
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of engineering and public health, spanning environmental and occupational epidemiology, environmental health and safety, water-borne disease control, and occupational health risks.

faculty.name: Shafiqul Islam
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering; Professor of Water Diplomacy, The Fletcher School
faculty.summary: Studies the availability, access, and allocation of water in the context of climate change, health, and geopolitics through an interdisciplinary approach combining science, engineering, policy, and negotiation.

faculty.name: Daniel Kuchma
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research focuses on the design, behavior, and modeling of reinforced and prestressed concrete structures subject to complex states of stress, including strut-and-tie methods and shear design of high-strength concrete bridge girders. Also works on foundations for offshore wind turbines.

faculty.name: Jonathan Lamontagne
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research addresses hydrologic extremes, water resources in a changing world, energy systems modeling, and robust adaptive planning, centering on uncertainty quantification in environmental and water systems.

faculty.name: Babak Moaveni
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Chair, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Works on Bayesian inference and inverse problems, structural health monitoring, and system and damage identification of civil structures, along with earthquake engineering and uncertainty quantification.

faculty.name: Masoud Sanayei
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Specializes in bridge structural health monitoring, developing methods for assessing structural condition using nondestructive test data, with additional work on train-induced building vibrations, fatigue prediction, and full-scale structural evaluation.

faculty.name: Lauryn Spearing
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Uses systems thinking to examine infrastructure management during uncertain contexts and sustainable water technology adoption, combining qualitative and quantitative methods at the intersection of technical, social, and natural systems.

faculty.name: Helen Suh
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Internationally recognized expert studying the impact of air pollutant exposures on dementia, cognitive performance, and cardiovascular health, and developing data modeling methods to predict air pollutant concentrations and assess extreme weather effects on health.

faculty.name: Deborah Sunter
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Focuses on computational modeling of electrical grid integration of renewable energy and storage, and explores the intersection of technology innovation and policy for environmental sustainability and energy justice.

faculty.name: Chris Swan
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering; Associate Dean of Inclusive Excellence, School of Engineering
faculty.summary: Examines sustainable development and innovative engineering education, with focus on waste minimization and reuse of traditional waste materials and on assessing service-based learning in engineering programs.

faculty.name: Farshid Vahedifard
faculty.title: Professor and Louis Berger Chair, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies the resilience and adaptation of critical infrastructure such as levees and dams to extreme events in a changing climate, with emphasis on disadvantaged communities and multi-physics processes in geotechnical engineering.

faculty.name: Robert Viesca
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Applies theoretical mechanics to problems in earth sciences and engineering, examining processes including landsliding, fault rupture, and seafloor sedimentation through the lens of friction, fracture, and deformation mechanics.

programs.program_name: PhD in Offshore Wind Energy Engineering

funding.note: Full-time PhD students in School of Engineering often receive tuition scholarship

faculty.name: Eric Hines
faculty.title: Professor of the Practice and Kentaro Tsutsumi Faculty Fellow, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Directs Tufts' offshore wind energy graduate program and has over 25 years of experience engineering innovative infrastructure. His work spans offshore wind structural design, earthquake engineering, and systems-level design at the technology-policy interface, including transmission expansion planning.

faculty.name: Daniel Kuchma
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Offshore Wind Foundations and Concrete Structures
faculty.summary: Global leader in structural testing methods who heads projects on corrosion and fatigue of offshore wind foundations and leads Tufts' efforts on nature-inclusive design. Combines experience in earthquake engineering and offshore oil and gas to extend wind turbine foundation service lives.

faculty.name: Babak Moaveni
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Chair, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Digital Twins and Structural Health Monitoring
faculty.summary: Leads Tufts' work on digital twinning of offshore wind turbines, combining physics-based and AI approaches. His broader research covers Bayesian inference and inverse problems, structural health monitoring, and damage identification of civil structures.

faculty.name: Barbara Kates-Garnick
faculty.title: Professor of the Practice in Energy Policy, The Fletcher School
faculty.summary: Leads the program's work on electricity transmission policy and researches energy transition, climate policy, and offshore wind policy. Former Undersecretary of Energy for Massachusetts, she collaborates with the School of Engineering on Offshore Wind Program research and state advisory efforts.

faculty.name: Beth Rosenberg
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Public Health and Community Medicine; Associate Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Offshore Wind Occupational Health and Safety
faculty.summary: Leads Tufts' transatlantic offshore wind safety initiative, investigating occupational and environmental health with emphasis on safety systems and culture. Recent work includes improving safety conditions in the offshore wind industry and workforce diversity, equity, and inclusion.

faculty.name: Laurie Gaskins Baise
faculty.title: Professor and Chair, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Works on natural hazards such as earthquakes and extreme wind, linking predictive models with observational data. Specializes in site response, regional hazard mapping, and remotely sensed damage assessment relevant to siting and foundations.

faculty.name: Masoud Sanayei
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Specializes in structural health monitoring of bridges and large structures, developing methods to assess structural condition from nondestructive test data, including fatigue prediction and full-scale structural evaluations.

faculty.name: Jonathan Lamontagne
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research addresses energy systems modeling, hydrologic extremes, and robust adaptive planning under deep uncertainty, centering on uncertainty quantification in coupled environmental and energy systems.

faculty.name: Deborah Sunter
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Focuses on computational modeling of electrical grid integration of renewable energy and storage, and explores the intersection of technology innovation and policy for environmental sustainability and energy justice.

faculty.name: Farshid Vahedifard
faculty.title: Professor and Louis Berger Chair, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies the resilience and adaptation of critical infrastructure to extreme events in a changing climate, with emphasis on multi-physics processes in geotechnical engineering relevant to energy infrastructure foundations.

funding.note: Full tuition scholarship plus stipend/research assistantship (Basic Science Division)

faculty.name: Lisa Alvarez-Cohen
faculty.title: Fred and Claire Sauer Professor; Vice Provost for Academic Planning
faculty.summary: Studies environmental microbiology and the biotransformation of contaminants, including biological processes for treating pollutants in water and wastewater systems.

faculty.name: Joshua Apte
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Researches air quality and atmospheric aerosols, examining human exposure to air pollution from neighborhood to global scales and climate change mitigation.

faculty.name: Maya Carrasquillo
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on urban water infrastructure and green stormwater systems with an emphasis on infrastructure justice and equitable community outcomes.

faculty.name: Fotini Katopodes Chow
faculty.title: Fred & Claire Sauer Chancellor's Chair; Professor
faculty.summary: Uses large-eddy simulation to study atmospheric flows, wind energy, urban pollutant dispersion, and air quality.

faculty.name: Paolo D'Odorico
faculty.title: Thomas J. Graff Professor of Natural Resources
faculty.summary: Studies surface hydrology, ecosystem ecology, aeolian processes, and desertification, linking water dynamics to ecological and environmental change.

faculty.name: Cynthia Gerlein-Safdi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates ecohydrology using remote sensing, satellite experiments, and stable isotopes to understand plant-water and land-surface interactions.

faculty.name: Allen Goldstein
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches atmospheric chemistry, terrestrial biogeochemistry, and biosphere-atmosphere exchange of trace gases and particles.

faculty.name: Robert Harley
faculty.title: Carl W. Johnson Professor in Civil Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies air pollution and air quality, including atmospheric chemistry and the impact of motor vehicle emissions on urban air.

faculty.name: Arpad Horvath
faculty.title: Edward G. Cahill and John R. Cahill Professor of Civil Engineering
faculty.summary: Applies life cycle assessment to infrastructure systems to evaluate and improve environmental sustainability.

faculty.name: Cesunica Ivey
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Vice Chair of Strategic Planning
faculty.summary: Focuses on air pollution assessment, community-centered resilience, and environmental justice.

faculty.name: Baoxia Mi
faculty.title: Wood-Calvert Chair in Engineering; Professor
faculty.summary: Develops membrane separation, desalination, and solar evaporation technologies for water purification.

faculty.name: Kara L. Nelson
faculty.title: Blum Chancellor's Chair in Development Engineering; Professor
faculty.summary: Researches pathogen detection and removal, water reuse, and nutrient recovery for sustainable water and sanitation systems.

faculty.name: Amy Pickering
faculty.title: Blum Center Distinguished Chair in Global Poverty and Practice; Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies environmental disease transmission, public health surveillance, and antimicrobial resistance related to water and sanitation.

faculty.name: David L. Sedlak
faculty.title: Plato Malozemoff Professor; Director, Berkeley Water Center
faculty.summary: Researches water reuse, urban water infrastructure, and treatment technologies including engineered treatment wetlands.

faculty.name: Mark Stacey
faculty.title: Henry and Joyce Miedema Professor of Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies coastal and estuarine physics and ecology, including climate adaptation of shoreline and estuarine systems.

faculty.name: Evan A. Variano
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches environmental fluid dynamics, focusing on mixing and transport by turbulence in aquatic environments.

faculty.name: Jonathan D. Bray
faculty.title: Faculty Chair in Earthquake Engineering Excellence; Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Works on geotechnical earthquake engineering using physical and numerical modeling of soil and foundation behavior under seismic loading.

faculty.name: Adda Athanasopoulos-Zekkos
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Vice Chair for Diversity, Equity, Inclusion & Belonging
faculty.summary: Researches geotechnical engineering of infrastructure and soil dynamics, including seismic response of geosystems.

faculty.name: Dimitrios Zekkos
faculty.title: Professor; Vice Chair for Strategic Planning and Implementation
faculty.summary: Studies geotechnical engineering with a focus on landslides and natural hazards affecting infrastructure.

faculty.name: Matthew DeJong
faculty.title: Ray & Shirley Clough Presidential Chair; Professor
faculty.summary: Researches earthquake engineering, infrastructure monitoring, and soil-structure interaction.

faculty.name: Khalid M. Mosalam
faculty.title: Taisei Professor of Civil Engineering; Director, Pacific Earthquake Engineering Research Center
faculty.summary: Works on structural health monitoring, experimental methods, and earthquake engineering for resilient structures.

faculty.name: James W. Rector
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches exploration geophysics and applied seismology, incorporating machine learning for subsurface imaging.

programs.program_name: Computational Precision Health PhD (joint with UCSF)

faculty.name: Justin Remais
faculty.title: Professor and Chair, Environmental Health Sciences Division
faculty.summary: Uses statistical and mathematical modeling to study how infectious diseases such as West Nile virus, TB, and schistosomiasis spread in response to urbanization, climate change, and shifting water resources.

faculty.name: Joshua Apte
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Investigates human exposure to air pollution from neighborhood to global scales, including how historical policies such as redlining drive disparities in air quality exposure.

faculty.name: Rachel Morello-Frosch
faculty.title: Professor, Environmental Health Sciences; Community Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Examines the social determinants of environmental health disparities and how inequality and psychosocial stress interact with chemical exposures, often using community-based participatory research.

faculty.name: Ellen Eisen
faculty.title: Professor in Residence, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Applies biostatistical and epidemiological methods to occupational and environmental health, addressing healthy worker survivor bias and workplace contributions to chronic disease.

faculty.name: Jay Graham
faculty.title: Associate Professor in Residence, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Applies epidemiologic methods and DNA sequencing to understand antimicrobial resistance and zoonotic infectious disease transmission across global regions.

faculty.name: Laura Kwong
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies environmental contamination and infectious disease exposure affecting child and maternal health in low-income countries, developing and evaluating engineering interventions through randomized controlled trials.

faculty.name: Ajay Pillarisetti
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Investigates how household energy consumption affects air pollution exposure, human health, and climate change, with a focus on low- and middle-income countries.

faculty.name: David J.X. González
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies how pollution from extractive industries and climate-driven disasters affects public health, using population-level data and community-engaged methods to address health disparities.

faculty.name: Misbath Daouda
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Examines the distribution of health benefits from energy transitions in the context of climate change in the US and West Africa using environmental epidemiology and exposure modeling.

faculty.name: Rosemarie de la Rosa
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies how social and environmental factors in childhood become biologically embedded and affect susceptibility to pollutant exposures, including combined effects of psychosocial stress and air pollution.

faculty.name: Bambarendage Pinithi Upekka Perera
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies how environmental toxicants alter the epigenome during critical developmental stages, integrating molecular biology and toxicology to examine maternal-fetal interactions and sex-specific epigenetic regulation.

faculty.name: Carly Hyland
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Cooperative Extension, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Investigates how climate change affects agricultural and food systems workers through interconnected exposures like heat, wildfire smoke, and pesticides, using community-engaged and mixed methods.

faculty.name: David Ackerly
faculty.title: Dean of Rausser College of Natural Resources and Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates climate change effects on California biodiversity, including distribution modeling, long-term vegetation dynamics, drought tolerance of native trees, and post-fire forest dynamics.

faculty.name: Justin Brashares
faculty.title: Professor and G.R. & W.M. Goertz Chair
faculty.summary: Studies how wildlife consumption and habitat conversion affect animal communities and population persistence, integrating ecology with economic, political, and cultural factors for conservation.

faculty.name: Carl Boettiger
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on ecological forecasting and decision-making under uncertainty for conservation and natural resource management, especially in systems prone to sudden regime shifts.

faculty.name: Timothy Bowles
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates soil health, climate resilience, and sustainable farming systems, examining how biodiversity-based agroecological approaches affect soil and climate adaptation.

faculty.name: Benjamin Blonder
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies plant responses to climate change through forest ecophysiological and genetic analyses, community assembly theory, and responses to heatwaves across temporal scales.

faculty.name: Stephanie Carlson
faculty.title: Professor and A.S. Leopold Chair in Wildlife Biology
faculty.summary: Studies the evolutionary ecology and conservation of freshwater fishes, focusing on the dynamics of California's inland fishes and factors shaping their persistence.

faculty.name: Todd Dawson
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates how plants interact with their environments using physiological plant ecology, stable isotope biogeochemistry, and ecohydrology, emphasizing woody plants and forest systems.

faculty.name: Iryna Dronova
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses remote sensing and spatial analysis to study ecosystem and landscape ecology, with emphasis on wetland biodiversity, phenological patterns, and urban green spaces.

faculty.name: Alejandra Echeverri
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Conservation Science
faculty.summary: Investigates how human behaviors such as habitat conversion affect wildlife, combining ornithological fieldwork with social science to advance biocultural conservation in the Neotropics.

faculty.name: Trevor Keenan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how terrestrial ecosystems respond to climate change and variability, focusing on carbon cycling, water use, and atmosphere feedbacks using large datasets, models, and field studies.

faculty.name: Céline Pallud
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Examines the transport and fate of chemical species in soils and sediments, emphasizing microbial processes affecting contaminant mobility using field, laboratory, and modeling approaches.

faculty.name: Albert Ruhi
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates how freshwater ecosystems respond to global change, particularly hydrologic alteration and drought, studying community dynamics, biodiversity, and regime shifts in river food webs.

faculty.name: Christopher Schell
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how urban carnivores such as coyotes and foxes adapt to city life, integrating behavioral and physiological approaches with research on how systemic racism shapes urban ecosystems.

faculty.name: Whendee Silver
faculty.title: Professor of Ecosystem Ecology and Biogeochemistry
faculty.summary: Studies how climate change and human activities affect biogeochemical cycling, focusing on carbon sequestration in grasslands, peatlands, and tropical forests, including the Marin Carbon Project.

faculty.name: Scott Stephens
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies wildland fire's interactions with ecosystems, including prehistoric fire regimes, current impacts, fire policy, and Indigenous-led ecosystem stewardship under climate change.

faculty.name: Lucas Vargas Zeppetello
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates land-atmosphere interactions, climate variability, and ecosystem responses to climate change.

faculty.name: Ian Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how environmental variation drives genetic, phenotypic, and ecological diversity across space and time through landscape genomics and evolutionary biology.

faculty.name: Manuela Girotto
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies hydrologic response and human-natural process interactions using land surface remote sensing and data assimilation, including snow hydrology and hydrology's contribution to sea-level change.

faculty.name: Miranda Redmond
faculty.title: Associate Professor in Forest Science and Climate Change
faculty.summary: Investigates how climate and disturbances affect forest dynamics to inform land management, encompassing tree demography, forest disturbances, and ecological forecasting.

faculty.name: John Battles
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates forest ecosystem dynamics through quantitative field studies, examining how disturbance and recovery cycles shape forest trajectories under anthropogenic change.

programs.program_name: Biochemistry, Molecular, Cellular and Developmental Biology

faculty.name: Mo Li
faculty.title: Professor and Chair, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies unconventional infrastructure materials and their interfaces with structural engineering, advanced manufacturing, spatial damage sensing, and the environment.

faculty.name: Mohammad Javad Abdolhosseini Qomi
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Researches the mechanics and physics of multi-scale porous materials, nano- and meso-chemo-mechanics, and engineering advanced materials for sustainable infrastructures.

faculty.name: Amir AghaKouchak
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering; Director of the Center for Hydrometeorology and Remote Sensing
faculty.summary: Works on hydrology, climatology, remote sensing of the environment, climate extremes, the water-energy nexus, climate change, stochastic modeling, and water resources management.

faculty.name: Tirtha Banerjee
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies atmospheric boundary layer dynamics, turbulent fluid dynamics, land/water/vegetation-atmosphere interactions, wildfires, vegetation dynamics, carbon and water cycles, and wetlands.

faculty.name: Elisa Borowski
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Researches transportation equity and justice, shared mobility, community needs and impacts, and travel behavior during extreme events.

faculty.name: Matthew Dean
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Works on sustainable transportation and energy systems, transportation-energy modeling and decision-making, and shared automated vehicles.

faculty.name: Russell Detwiler
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies groundwater hydrology, contaminant fate and transport, subsurface process modeling, and groundwater/surface-water interaction.

faculty.name: Efi Foufoula-Georgiou
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Researches hydrology and geomorphology with emphasis on modeling the interactions between the atmosphere, land, and the terrestrial environment at plot to large-watershed scale.

faculty.name: Shakira Hobbs
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering; Samueli Faculty Development Chair
faculty.summary: Focuses on global sustainability, resource recovery from anthropogenic waste, and life cycle thinking applied to engineering equity at the food-energy-water nexus.

faculty.name: Kuo-Lin Hsu
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Works on remote sensing of precipitation, hydrologic systems modeling, stochastic hydrology, and water resources systems planning.

faculty.name: Michael Hyland
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Researches the modeling, design, control, and analysis of smart city transportation systems, with emphases on shared-use autonomous mobility services and urban transit networks.

faculty.name: R. Jayakrishnan
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Specializes in transportation systems analysis.

faculty.name: Sunny Jiang
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering; Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies water pollution microbiology, environmental biotechnology, and aquatic microbial ecology.

faculty.name: Wenlong Jin
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Researches intelligent transportation systems, traffic flow theory, and transportation network analysis.

faculty.name: Anne Lemnitzer
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Works on geotechnical and earthquake engineering, soil-structure interaction, reinforced concrete design, and seismic monitoring.

faculty.name: Michael McNally
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering; Urban Planning and Public Policy
faculty.summary: Studies travel behavior and transportation systems analysis.

faculty.name: Ayman Mosallam
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering; Materials & Manufacturing Engineering Technology
faculty.summary: Researches advanced composites and hybrid systems, seismic repair and rehabilitation of structures, structural health monitoring, 3D printing in construction, and sustainable building technology.

faculty.name: Christopher Olivares Martinez
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Works on environmental biotechnology, remediation, metabolomics, pollutant fate in natural and engineered systems, microbial toxicity, and environmental organic chemistry.

faculty.name: Diego Rosso
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering; Materials Science and Engineering; Director of the UCI WEX Center
faculty.summary: Studies environmental process engineering, mass transfer, wastewater treatment, and carbon- and energy-footprint analysis.

faculty.name: Brett Sanders
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering; Urban Planning and Public Policy
faculty.summary: Researches urban, coastal, and dam-break flooding, flood hazard modeling and risk management, sediment management, shallow-water hydrodynamics and morphodynamics, computational methods, and drone-based remote sensing.

faculty.name: Jean-Daniel Saphores
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering; Economics; Planning, Policy, and Design; Director, Institute of Transportation Studies-Irvine
faculty.summary: Studies transportation and environmental systems with a focus on air pollution and energy use, travel behavior analysis, alternative fuel and automated vehicles, transit use, sustainable infrastructure management, and decision making under uncertainty.

faculty.name: Soroosh Sorooshian
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor and Samueli Endowed Chair in Engineering, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering; Earth System Science; Founding Director of th
faculty.summary: Works on hydrology, hydrometeorology and hydroclimate modeling, remote sensing, and water resources management.

faculty.name: Lizhi Sun
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Researches micro- and nano-mechanics, composites and nanocomposites, smart materials and structures, multiscale modeling, and elastography.

faculty.name: Jasper Vrugt
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering; Earth System Science
faculty.summary: Examines how (eco)systems work, why theories deviate from data, and how scientists diagnose change, as applied to biogeosciences, ecology, geomorphology, geophysics, hydrology, and soils.

faculty.name: Farzin Zareian
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Works on structural engineering, performance-based earthquake engineering, structural reliability, and structural control.

faculty.name: Scott Bartell
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health; Vice Dean, Academic Affairs
faculty.summary: Has dedicated his research to quantifying human exposures and health effects caused by environmental contaminants, including perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS), with a focus on contamination and epidemiology in U.S. water sources.

faculty.name: Stephen Bondy
faculty.title: Professor of Medicine, Environmental & Occupational Health
faculty.summary: His work focuses on brain aging and the environmental and dietary agents that promote or retard such aging.

faculty.name: Saurabh Chatterjee
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health; Professor of Medicine, UCI School of Medicine
faculty.summary: Investigates host-microbiome interactions with redox biology, neuroimmune pathology, and gut-brain interactions in pro-inflammatory disease processes, including Gulf War illness and chronic multi-symptom illnesses.

faculty.name: Andrea De Vizcaya Ruiz
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health; Graduate Program Director, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: An environmental toxicologist who focuses on the effects of particulate matter, microplastics, and nanomaterials toxicity on the human body, using laboratory and animal models to evaluate tissue injury, inflammation, and oxidative stress from environmental and occupational exposures.

faculty.name: Rufus Edwards
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health
faculty.summary: Investigates how emissions affect populations globally, examining exposures to combustion byproducts in industrialized nations and household solid fuel emissions in less industrialized regions; a widely cited expert on solid fuels used for cooking and heating.

faculty.name: Jill Johnston
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health
faculty.summary: Her research centers on community-driven approaches to understand health impacts from industrial pollution and energy production, partnering with local organizations and residents on air pollution, oil and gas extraction, extreme heat, and environmental justice issues.

faculty.name: Masashi Kitazawa
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health
faculty.summary: Investigates the molecular pathogenesis of neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's disease and how environmental toxicants affect neuroinflammation, examining cellular signaling pathways in brain immune cells using experimental models and cultured cells.

faculty.name: Karen Lincoln
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental and Occupational Health; Director of Center for Environmental Health Disparities Research
faculty.summary: Investigates psychosocial, behavioral, and biological mechanisms that link social determinants to health and well-being, focusing on improving outcomes for Black Americans, older adults, and marginalized populations.

faculty.name: Ulrike Luderer
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health; Director, Center for Occupational & Environmental Health
faculty.summary: A reproductive toxicologist focused on how toxicants and ionizing radiation disrupt reproductive function and on mechanisms that protect against such disruption, including effects on ovarian health across generations.

faculty.name: Marizen Ramirez
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health; Associate Dean for Diversity, Equity & Inclusion
faculty.summary: Specializes in injury and violence prevention, examining factors that influence injury rates among workers and exploring community-based approaches to preventing violence.

faculty.name: David Richardson
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health; Associate Dean of Research
faculty.summary: Designs studies to explore potential occupational health hazards so that employers and policymakers can construct safer working environments, with interests including radiation exposure and cancer risk, heat-related worker injury, and novel epidemiological methods.

faculty.name: Nicole Sparks
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health
faculty.summary: Investigates how environmental toxicants, particularly tobacco product exposure, affect fetal development, using stem cell and animal models to examine changes in microRNA and mRNA expression that cause embryonic bone-cell defects.

faculty.name: Veronica Vieira
faculty.title: Chair & Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health
faculty.summary: Conducts epidemiologic analyses of health data to examine the contributions of known risk factors and environmental exposures to underlying patterns of disease risk, using geographic methods to model environmental exposures and identify communities facing elevated health risks.

faculty.name: Jun Wu
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health; Director, Graduate Programs in Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Examines influences of various environmental exposures, such as air pollution, climate, and the built environment, on reproductive and children's health outcomes through population-based epidemiological studies.

faculty.name: Matthew Alford
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates internal waves, ocean mixing, and coastal oceanographic phenomena, developing ocean instrumentation and technology to study upper-ocean and submesoscale processes.

faculty.name: Paola Cessi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies global ocean circulation and hydrography using theoretical and computational approaches to understand the dynamics of oceanic and atmospheric systems.

faculty.name: Ian Eisenman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on climate dynamics and the role of ice in the climate system, including modeling and analysis of past climate change and physical oceanography processes.

faculty.name: Falk Feddersen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on coastal oceanography and ocean wave dynamics, including internal waves, ocean mixing, and nonlinear surface waves, along with modeling and state estimation of oceanic systems.

faculty.name: Sarah Giddings
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates interactions between estuaries and the coastal ocean, focusing on estuarine dynamics and bio-physical processes that shape coastal environments.

faculty.name: Sarah Gille
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on physical oceanography, climate sciences, and ocean-atmosphere interactions, with particular emphasis on Southern Ocean dynamics and autonomous ocean observing systems.

faculty.name: Shaun Johnston
faculty.title: Researcher
faculty.summary: Studies small-scale ocean dynamics using autonomous platforms such as gliders and profiling floats, with focus areas including internal tide behavior, coastal fronts and eddies, and observations under tropical cyclones.

faculty.name: Jennifer MacKinnon
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in physical oceanography with a focus on internal waves, ocean mixing, and coastal oceanographic processes, spanning applied ocean sciences and upper-ocean dynamics.

faculty.name: Matthew Mazloff
faculty.title: Researcher
faculty.sub_field: Ocean State Estimation and Southern Ocean
faculty.summary: Works on ocean modeling, climate dynamics, and physical oceanography, with interests spanning autonomous ocean platforms, biogeochemistry, ocean circulation, and Southern Ocean processes.

faculty.name: Mark Merrifield
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on coastal oceanographic phenomena, including internal waves, ocean mixing processes, and nonlinear surface wave dynamics and how they shape coastal environments.

faculty.name: Sarah Purkey
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on ocean observation and the climate system, spanning autonomous platforms, global hydrography, and high-latitude climate research, connecting ocean circulation patterns to broader climate questions.

faculty.name: Daniel Rudnick
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans physical oceanography with a focus on autonomous platforms, ocean circulation, internal waves, and ocean-atmosphere interactions, addressing coastal and global processes with advanced observing systems.

faculty.name: Lia Siegelman
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on physical oceanography, upper-ocean processes, and ocean-atmosphere interactions, with particular emphasis on submesoscale dynamics and Southern Ocean climate studies.

faculty.name: Janet Sprintall
faculty.title: Researcher
faculty.summary: Studies ocean circulation patterns and hydrography, examining interactions between the ocean and atmosphere across tropical and high-latitude climate regions.

faculty.name: Amy Waterhouse
faculty.title: Research Scientist
faculty.summary: Investigates internal waves and global patterns of ocean mixing, examining how submarine canyons modify internal wave energy, with additional focus on coastal physical oceanography in estuarine environments.

faculty.name: Shang-Ping Xie
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on climate sciences, physical oceanography, and tropical meteorology, using modeling of coupled oceanic and atmospheric systems to understand climate dynamics.

faculty.name: Amato Evan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on atmospheric aerosols, climate sciences, ocean-atmosphere interactions, remote sensing, and tropical meteorology, examining connections between atmospheric and oceanic processes.

faculty.name: Nick Lutsko
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works on modeling and understanding the coupled dynamics of the ocean, atmosphere, and climate system.

faculty.name: Joel Norris
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Conducts research in climate sciences, cloud physics and boundary layer processes, and ocean-atmosphere interactions.

faculty.name: Katharine Ricke
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on climate sciences, ocean-atmosphere interactions, and ocean acidification, encompassing biogeochemistry, greenhouse gases, and modeling of oceanic and atmospheric systems.

faculty.name: Duncan Watson-Parris
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Conducts research in climate and atmospheric science at Scripps, holding a joint appointment with the Halicioglu Data Science Institute and applying data-driven methods to climate problems.

faculty.name: Luc Lenain
faculty.title: Associate Research Scientist
faculty.summary: Investigates air-sea interactions using laboratory experiments, field measurements, and numerical modeling, focusing on wave breaking, sea-spray generation, and turbulence in the atmospheric and oceanic boundary layers.

faculty.name: Drew Lucas
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates internal waves and ocean mixing processes, as well as upper-ocean and submesoscale dynamics, focusing on physical processes that shape marine environments.

faculty.name: John Cottle
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Applies geochronology to solve tectonic problems, combining isotopic dating with field studies of continental tectonics.

faculty.name: Zach Eilon
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Seismologist working on seismic tomography and inverse methods, using anisotropy and attenuation to image Earth structure and tectonic processes.

faculty.name: Tobias Fischer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Volcanologist focusing on volatiles in active volcanic and hydrothermal systems.

faculty.name: Phillip Gans
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies continental structural geology and tectonics, including geochronology of deformed terranes.

faculty.name: Matt Jackson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses stable and radiogenic isotope systems to study the geochemical evolution of the Earth's mantle and crust.

faculty.name: Chen Ji
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Characterizes earthquake sources using seismological and geodetic observations, including finite-fault rupture modeling.

faculty.name: Gen Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the chemical and physical processes that cycle materials and elements in the Earth system, linking surface processes to geochemical cycles.

faculty.name: Lorraine Lisiecki
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Paleoclimatologist known for correlation of paleoclimate records and development of age models, including benthic isotope stacks used to reconstruct glacial cycles.

faculty.name: Carolina Martinez Gutierrez
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Applies genomics and phylogenomics to study the evolution of life, including the early origins of marine bacteria and archaea.

faculty.name: Robin Matoza
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies volcano seismology, acoustics, and eruption dynamics using seismic and infrasound observations of active volcanoes.

faculty.name: Kristin Morell
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the dynamics of tectonically active regions, with an emphasis on subduction zones and active faulting.

faculty.name: Susannah Porter
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Paleobiologist studying the rise of complex life, from early eukaryotes to the first skeletal animals.

faculty.name: Morgan Raven
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies organic matter preservation and organic sulfur cycling in marine sediments and the modern and ancient carbon cycle.

faculty.name: Roberta L. Rudnick
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Geochemist and petrologist studying the formation and evolution of the continents and the composition of the continental crust and lithosphere.

faculty.name: Alex Simms
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Studies Quaternary marine settings and the reconstruction of past sea-level change from coastal stratigraphy.

faculty.name: David Valentine
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Combines geochemistry and microbiology to address ocean-related environmental problems, including biogeochemical cycling of methane and petroleum in the ocean.
