faculty.name: Doug Crowe
faculty.title: Professor, Associate Department Head
faculty.summary: Applies conventional and laser microprobe stable isotope techniques to economic geology studies of ore deposits and fluid systems. Recent collaborative work extends into geobiological and paleoclimatic investigations, including hot spring environments and archaeological applications of isotope geochemistry.

faculty.name: Avishek Dutta
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Leads a lab exploring microbial life in extreme environments to inform understanding of the origins of life and potential extraterrestrial biology. His applied research develops microbially driven solutions for bioremediation, biomining, and recovery of critical metals from waste streams.

faculty.name: Brennan Ferguson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates biogeochemical processes affecting contaminant fate in subsurface environments using a multi-scale approach from benchtop lab experiments to field-scale experiments. Emphasizes interactions among organic matter, minerals, and radionuclide transport.

faculty.name: Charlotte Garing
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops novel laboratory methods using rock cores and micromodels to study fluid flow, multiphase transport, and mineral-fluid reactivity in porous media. Her research supports applications in groundwater systems, the vadose zone, and geologic carbon storage.

faculty.name: Steven Holland
faculty.title: Shellebarger Professor of Geology and Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Investigates how ecological communities and sedimentary environments respond to climate and sea-level change through fieldwork and computer simulations, applying sequence stratigraphy to understand how the fossil record is constructed. Recent focus is on nonmarine systems such as rivers and lakes. His faculty page states he is not presently accepting new graduate students.

faculty.name: Christian Klimczak
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the growth of faults and other fractures in active and ancient geological settings on Earth and other terrestrial planets. Integrates field observations, laboratory analysis, and remote sensing data to understand deformation mechanisms and planetary tectonic and volcanic evolution.

faculty.name: Adam Milewski
faculty.title: Professor & Department Head
faculty.summary: Uses field-based data and emerging techniques including remote sensing, UAVs, GIS, and machine learning to address water resource and environmental sustainability challenges, particularly in dryland hydrology. Recent work extends to decarbonization solutions such as carbon capture and storage and geologic hydrogen.

faculty.name: Valentine Nzengung
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops innovative environmental remediation technologies including phytoremediation, microbial mat treatment systems, biochar applications, and in-situ bioremediation of soils and groundwater. His work addresses contaminant degradation, metal immobilization, and sustainable treatment of hazardous waste sites; he is a National Academy of Inventors Fellow.

faculty.name: Mattia Pistone
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Combines experimental, analytical, and field-based geology to investigate how magmas behave during transport and eruption, including viscosity changes, volatile transfer, and melt segregation in volcanic arc systems. He is establishing the MAGMA MIA experimental laboratory at UGA and is co-PI on the ICDP DIVE drilling project. His UGA page advertises that he is seeking a highly motivated PhD-level graduate student to study gas retention and release in magmas.

faculty.name: Bruce Railsback
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates records of climate and environmental change in Earth's history through the petrography, mineralogy, and geochemistry of stalagmites and other carbonate materials. His work combines microscopic analysis with isotopic and chemical methods to reconstruct past climate across multiple continents.

faculty.name: Paul Schroeder
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates clay minerals and their crystal-chemical structures using spectroscopy, isotope analysis, and diffraction techniques. His research explores how these abundant natural materials interact with biological systems and record environmental change on Earth and in extraterrestrial environments.

faculty.name: Sally Walker
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how biological, physical, and chemical factors affect the preservation of marine organisms in modern and fossil deposits, with experimental taphonomy work spanning temperate, tropical, and polar regions. Current projects include Antarctic scallops as ecosystem engineers, middle Cambrian taphonomy, and the Middle Miocene climate transition.

faculty.name: David Fastovich
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how complex climate processes produce similarly complex ecological dynamics by examining the geologic record across multiple timescales. Uses organic biomarkers, isotope geochemistry, and proxy-based climate reconstructions.

faculty.name: Andrew J. Grundstein
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches climate and human health, heat vulnerability, and environmental monitoring, with emphasis on heat-related hazards including exertional heat illness in athletes, urban heat exposure, and occupational heat stress.

faculty.name: Anna Harper
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies interactions between vegetation, people, and climate, with emphasis on land management, carbon sinks, and ecosystem responses to environmental change. Applies satellite data and ground measurements to improve Earth system model climate projections, and contributes to the IPCC 7th Assessment Report.

faculty.name: Weiming Hu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Geospatial Data Science & Environmental Uncertainty Quantification
faculty.summary: Quantifies and understands uncertainty from multi-source data including remote sensing, model simulations, and observations. Develops accurate and reliable machine learning models for environmental and Earth sciences, applied to renewable energy forecasting, extreme event forecasting, and water resource management.

faculty.name: John A. Knox
faculty.title: Josiah Meigs Distinguished Teaching Professor
faculty.summary: Researches the dynamics of weather and climate, geoscience education, atmospheric hazards, and meteorological applications of social media data. Serves as Associate Director and Undergraduate Coordinator of the Atmospheric Sciences Program.

faculty.name: Gabriel Kooperman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: His research group answers critical questions about Earth's changing climate system that impact freshwater availability, sustainability of natural ecosystems, and environmental extremes such as floods, droughts, air quality, and heat waves, using computational climate modeling across multiple scales.

faculty.name: David S. Leigh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Geomorphologist specializing in late Quaternary climate changes and human impacts throughout the Holocene, with expertise in fluvial and eolian systems, hillslope processes, soils, paleofloods, and geoarchaeology across the Americas and the Pyrenees. His profile states he is currently recruiting students in Quaternary environmental change, geoarchaeology, paleoflood analysis, river channel change, and geomorphic consequences of dam removal.

faculty.name: Paul Miller
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Concentrates on tropical meteorology and climatology, with particular emphasis on Saharan dust transport across the north Atlantic Ocean. Also investigates hurricane impacts and drought processes in the U.S. Caribbean and Gulf Coast.

faculty.name: Deepak R. Mishra
faculty.title: Merle C. Prunty, Jr. Professor and Department Head
faculty.summary: Monitors fragile coastal and inland water ecosystems by combining field spectroscopy with satellite remote sensing, including satellite-based decision support tools for salt marsh conservation, cyanobacterial harmful algal bloom mapping, tidal wetland carbon sequestration, and seagrass mapping. Directs the Center for Geospatial Research and the UGA Small Satellite Research Laboratory.

faculty.name: Thomas Mote
faculty.title: Distinguished Research Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates atmospheric circulation's influence on precipitation and ice ablation extremes, including over two decades studying the role of atmospheric blocking and atmospheric rivers on the surface mass balance of the Greenland ice sheet. Recent work examines climate, land cover change, and aerosol impacts on Caribbean precipitation and drought.

faculty.name: Lan Mu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on Geographic Information Science and applies geospatial methods to health disparities and environmental challenges, including spatial analysis of healthcare access, geovisualization, computational geometry, and AI applications in geographic problem-solving. Directs the GIScience Certificate Program and the GIS.HEAL lab.

faculty.name: David F. Porinchu
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Department Head
faculty.summary: Broadly trained biogeographer specializing in Quaternary science, paleoclimatology, and paleoecology. Directs the Environmental Change Laboratory, using lake sediment properties to reconstruct long-term and recent patterns of climate and environmental change in arctic and alpine regions of North America, Central America, and Eurasia.

faculty.name: Fausto O. Sarmiento
faculty.title: Professor of Mountain Science
faculty.summary: Research focuses on montology, Neotropical mountains, critical biogeography, political ecology, and socioecological landscapes and seascapes, with current work on climate change adaptation, mountainscape conservation, and restoration of Tropandean landscapes. Directs the Neotropical Montology Collaboratory.

faculty.name: J. Marshall Shepherd
faculty.title: Georgia Athletic Association Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on hydrometeorological extremes, urban climate, and the intersections of atmospheric sciences with society, using remote sensing, weather-climate modeling, and risk-vulnerability approaches applied to urban flooding, the energy-food-water nexus, and risk communication. Directs the UGA Atmospheric Sciences Program.

faculty.name: Suzanne Pilaar Birch
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Geography and Anthropology
faculty.summary: Investigates human adaptation and resilience to climate change and natural resource unpredictability in prehistory, combining archaeological and biogeochemical approaches to study diet, mobility, and settlement from the end of the last ice age through early farming. Directs the Quaternary Isotope Paleoecology Laboratory.

faculty.name: Xiaobai (Angela) Yao
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in geospatial data science at the intersection of GIScience, human dynamics, and GeoAI, integrating geospatial analytics with machine-learning/AI techniques for urban transportation and public health applications. Her profile states she is always happy to recruit promising graduate students interested in geospatial analytical techniques for urban and human dynamics.

requirements.admission_model: Interdisciplinary umbrella admissions program with lab rotations

faculty.name: Travis C. Glenn
faculty.title: Department Head, Dean Phillip Williams Professor, Director, Institute of Bioinformatics
faculty.summary: Develops and applies genomic tools to study evolutionary biology and address environmental health and infectious disease ecology challenges. Work focuses particularly on tick vectors and disease transmission systems.

faculty.name: Erin K. Lipp
faculty.title: Associate Dean for Academic Affairs, Georgia Power Professor of Environmental Health, Director of Graduate Studies, EHS
faculty.summary: Her lab investigates microbial ecology and environmental microbiology at the interface of public and ecosystem health, spanning water sanitation, climate change, and coral disease. Current work focuses on bacterial pathogen population dynamics in aquatic systems.

faculty.name: Charles A. Easley, IV
faculty.title: Associate Dean for Research, Interim Director, Institute for Disaster Management, Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on the impacts of environmental exposures on spermatogenesis and future generations, and on developing stem cell treatments for male factor infertility. Work also includes identifying compounds for male contraceptive drug discovery.

faculty.name: Luke Naeher
faculty.title: Professor, Associate Department Head
faculty.summary: Faculty member in Environmental Health Science focused on exposure science and environmental air pollution. The directory profile does not provide a detailed research summary beyond his departmental affiliation.

faculty.name: Lili Tang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Her lab develops high-throughput C. elegans platforms combined with multi-omics and artificial intelligence to investigate neurotoxic mechanisms and host-microbe-xenobiotic interactions. Particular focus on microbial-based interventions for neurodegenerative diseases such as Parkinson's.

faculty.name: Jia-Sheng Wang
faculty.title: UGA Athletic Association Professor in Public Health
faculty.summary: Research focuses on molecular epidemiology and prevention of human esophageal cancer and hepatocellular carcinoma, examining how environmental and food-borne toxicants affect human health. Work includes developing biomarkers for carcinogen detection in high-risk populations.

faculty.name: Wentao Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: His laboratory investigates the role of DNA damage and repair in mutagenesis and carcinogenesis caused by environmental carcinogens. Work involves developing methods to detect environmentally-induced DNA damage and examining how cell types respond to toxic exposures.

faculty.name: Anne Marie Zimeri
faculty.title: Associate Professor, B.S.E.H. Program Director and Undergraduate Internship Coordinator
faculty.summary: Research focuses on environmental agent exposures from produce and water and on undergraduate environmental education. Her scholarly work emphasizes connecting scientific concepts to real-world applications in environmental health.

faculty.name: John Patrick McCracken
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates health effects of air pollution, particularly from use of solid fuels for cooking in low-income settings. Leads the Guatemalan research center within the multi-country Household Air Pollution Intervention Network (HAPIN) and studies respiratory disease in children.

faculty.name: José F. Cordero
faculty.title: Gordhan L. Virginia B. "Jinx" Patel Distinguished Professor of Public Health
faculty.summary: Investigates how endocrine disruptors and oxidative stress influence preterm births and neurodevelopment through large cohort studies in Puerto Rico, including the PROTECT study and CRECE center. Work also examines Zika infection risks during pregnancy via the ZIP consortium.

faculty.name: Larry Di Girolamo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Satellite remote sensing of clouds and aerosols
faculty.summary: Studies cloud, aerosol, and radiation processes using satellite remote sensing and 3-D radiative transfer modeling, characterizing their global properties and changes over the satellite era and their impacts on weather, climate, and human health.

faculty.name: Francina Dominguez
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Head, Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Her hydroclimatology research examines interactions between the land and the atmosphere, focusing on how climate change and human land modification affect hydrology and the reciprocal effects of surface hydrology changes on climate.

faculty.name: Kelvin K. Droegemeier
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: His work spans severe convective storm prediction and atmospheric remote sensing, including fine-scale computational models for earth system science and numerical prediction methods for severe weather events.

faculty.name: Deanna Hence
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Radar and satellite studies of convective and tropical weather
faculty.summary: Investigates impacts of hazardous weather on human systems, convective storm systems, tropical meteorology, and orographic precipitation, primarily through radar and satellite remote sensing, with an emphasis on bridging science with policy and public engagement.

faculty.name: Atul Jain
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Terrestrial biogeochemical cycles and land-use change modeling
faculty.summary: Leads global model-data integration studies of biogeochemical cycles and water and energy fluxes, investigating the impact of land use and land cover change on biogeophysics and biogeochemistry, greenhouse gas emissions, and climate change effects on agricultural production.

faculty.name: Steve Nesbitt
faculty.title: Professor and Department Head
faculty.sub_field: Radar and satellite meteorology of precipitation systems
faculty.summary: Leads a research group studying radar meteorology, satellite meteorology, tropical meteorology, mesoscale meteorology, and data science, focusing on convective storm processes and precipitation measurement using advanced observational techniques.

faculty.name: Cristian Proistosescu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates coupled atmosphere-ocean dynamics and how clouds and radiative processes shape climate feedbacks and climate sensitivity, combining modeling, observations, paleoclimate, and statistical inference across multiple timescales.

faculty.name: Shiv Priyam Raghuraman
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Earth's radiation budget and cloud feedbacks
faculty.summary: Investigates how Earth's planetary radiation balance is altered by clouds, aerosols, and other atmospheric constituents, combining satellite observations with climate models to understand how radiative changes cause Earth to accumulate heat, with focus on cloud feedbacks under surface warming.

faculty.name: Nicole Riemer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Her group develops computer models to track how aerosol particles form, move, and change in the atmosphere, combining simulations with observational data to examine aerosol effects on human health, weather, and climate.

faculty.name: Ryan Sriver
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works to develop a deeper understanding of the physical processes influencing variability within Earth's climate system and to quantify uncertainties in future projections, using observations, statistical methods, and numerical models to study extreme weather events and their drivers.

faculty.name: Robert Trapp
faculty.title: Professor and Director of the School of Earth, Society & Environment
faculty.summary: Investigates severe convective storms, examining their dynamics, associated hazards, and relationships to climate change; author of a textbook on mesoscale-convective atmospheric processes.

faculty.name: Joseph Trujillo-Falcón
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Examines how speakers of multiple languages comprehend and respond to severe weather and climate threats, bridging atmospheric science and communication through collaboration with practitioners and communities to improve risk communication technologies and strategies.

faculty.name: Zhuo Wang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates tropical meteorology and climate dynamics, including tropical cyclones, intraseasonal variability, tropical-extratropical interaction, and subseasonal to decadal forecasting, aiming to advance understanding of atmospheric dynamics and predictive capabilities.

faculty.name: Gan Zhang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: His group investigates climate risks involving physical phenomena such as cyclones and socioeconomic factors such as the energy transition, combining geophysical fluid dynamics with machine learning and real-world applications.
